content.text: Now let me read the weighted scheduler, multipath bind, and the example config to establish the default mode and how a single flow is handled.

content.content:
1	package sched
2	
3	import (
4		"fmt"
5		"math"
6		"sync"
7		"time"
8	
9		"github.com/7mind/wanbond/internal/log"
10		"github.com/7mind/wanbond/internal/telemetry"
11	)
12	
13	// WeightedConfig tunes the weighted-aggregation scheduler. The device builds it
14	// from config.SchedulerConfig; sched holds its own struct so the package does not
15	// depend on config. Every field is validated by NewWeighted (fail-fast at the
16	// composition root), mirroring the config-boundary validation.
17	type WeightedConfig struct {
18		// PerPathCapacity is the reference per-path capacity in frame-selection slots per
19		// second: the denominator the aggregation load-gate compares offered load against,
20		// and (when Pacing is on) the per-path token-bucket refill rate. There is no
21		// measured BDP (P0 §7), so capacity is expressed in the only unit the scheduler
22		// observes — Pick() invocations per second. Must be > 0.
23		PerPathCapacity float64
24		// EngageFraction engages aggregation once smoothed offered load exceeds
25		// EngageFraction*PerPathCapacity. In (0,1].
26		EngageFraction float64
27		// DisengageFraction collapses aggregation to primary-only once smoothed offered
28		// load stays below DisengageFraction*PerPathCapacity for CollapseDwell. In
29		// [0,EngageFraction): the gap is the hysteresis band that keeps the metered path
30		// from dribbling on/off around a single threshold (data-thrift, requirement 2).
31		DisengageFraction float64
32		// CollapseDwell is the sustained-low-load dwell before collapsing to primary-only
33		// (the temporal half of the hysteresis, mirroring the active-backup failback dwell).
34		CollapseDwell time.Duration
35		// LoadTau is the time constant of the exponentially-weighted offered-load rate
36		// estimator. Must be > 0.
37		LoadTau time.Duration
38	
39		// Pacing turns per-path send-pacing on. When false the token buckets are bypassed
40		// (a documented no-op: P0 §7 could not empirically size the pace in the unmetered
41		// fixture), but PerPathCapacity still drives the aggregation gate.
42		Pacing bool
43		// PacingBurst is the per-path token-bucket burst in frame slots. Must be > 0 when
44		// Pacing is on.
45		PacingBurst float64
46	
47		// WeightRTTFloor floors RTT in the weight formula so a cold path (no RTT samples
48		// yet, near-zero RTT) cannot be handed unbounded weight. Must be > 0.
49		WeightRTTFloor time.Duration
50		// WeightLossFloor floors the loss term under the square root so a zero-loss path
51		// gets a large-but-finite weight and two zero-loss paths split by inverse-RTT
52		// alone. Must be > 0.
53		WeightLossFloor float64
54	}
55	
56	func (c WeightedConfig) validate() error {
57		if c.PerPathCapacity <= 0 {
58			return fmt.Errorf("sched: weighted PerPathCapacity must be > 0, got %g", c.PerPathCapacity)
59		}
60		if c.EngageFraction <= 0 || c.EngageFraction > 1 {
61			return fmt.Errorf("sched: weighted EngageFraction must be in (0,1], got %g", c.EngageFraction)
62		}
63		if c.DisengageFraction < 0 || c.DisengageFraction >= c.EngageFraction {
64			return fmt.Errorf("sched: weighted DisengageFraction must be in [0,EngageFraction=%g), got %g", c.EngageFraction, c.DisengageFraction)
65		}
66		if c.CollapseDwell < 0 {
67			return fmt.Errorf("sched: weighted CollapseDwell must be >= 0, got %s", c.CollapseDwell)
68		}
69		if c.LoadTau <= 0 {
70			return fmt.Errorf("sched: weighted LoadTau must be > 0, got %s", c.LoadTau)
71		}
72		if c.Pacing && c.PacingBurst <= 0 {
73			return fmt.Errorf("sched: weighted PacingBurst must be > 0 when pacing is enabled, got %g", c.PacingBurst)
74		}
75		if c.WeightRTTFloor <= 0 {
76			return fmt.Errorf("sched: weighted WeightRTTFloor must be > 0, got %s", c.WeightRTTFloor)
77		}
78		if c.WeightLossFloor <= 0 {
79			return fmt.Errorf("sched: weighted WeightLossFloor must be > 0, got %g", c.WeightLossFloor)
80		}
81		return nil
82	}
83	
84	// FECPolicy is the P3+ forward-error-correction extension seam. Given the path a
85	// frame was scheduled onto and the current eligible set, it returns ADDITIONAL path
86	// indices the frame should ALSO egress on for redundancy (e.g. a repair copy on a
87	// second path). This is a documented hook, NOT an implementation: the current
88	// single-path Scheduler.Pick returns one index, so wiring redundant transmission
89	// requires the P3 Bind change that fans a frame out to several sockets. Until then
90	// a nil policy adds no redundancy and RedundantPaths returns nothing, so the seam is
91	// inert but present and unit-tested.
92	type FECPolicy interface {
93		RedundantPaths(chosen int, eligible []int) []int
94	}
95	
96	// WeightedScheduler is the T21 send-side scheduler: a weighted-aggregation policy
97	// that, under load, stripes a single flow across every eligible path in proportion
98	// to a per-path RTT/loss-derived weight, and at low load COLLAPSES to the primary
99	// (index 0) so the metered backup path stays ~idle (data-thrift, requirement 2). It
100	// engages the backup only once offered load exceeds one path's capacity and
101	// disengages with hysteresis (a two-threshold band plus a collapse dwell) so the
102	// metered path never dribbles on/off. Per-path send-pacing (token buckets) bounds
103	// each path's egress rate so aggregation does not build a standing queue that
104	// inflates latency-under-load (P0 §7); pacing is a config no-op when disabled.
105	//
106	// It reads per-path liveness from PathHealth (the T13 up/down verdict) and per-path
107	// quality from PathQuality (the T13 Estimate: RTT/loss). In production BOTH are the
108	// SAME *telemetry.Prober per path; the unit tests drive synthetic sources.
109	//
110	// Weight formula (Mathis-style throughput proxy). A single steady-state TCP flow's
111	// bandwidth is ∝ 1/(RTT·√p) (Mathis et al.); used here as a RELATIVE path-quality
112	// proxy it directs more of the aggregate flow onto the path a flow would run faster
113	// on. Per eligible path:
114	//
115	//	w_i = 1 / ( max(RTT_i, RTTFloor) · sqrt(Loss_i + LossFloor) )
116	//
117	// normalized over the eligible set. RTTFloor bounds a cold/zero-RTT path; LossFloor
118	// keeps a zero-loss path finite (two zero-loss paths then split by inverse-RTT
119	// alone). There is no measured bandwidth to use (Estimate is RTT/Jitter/Loss only,
120	// P0 §7), so an RTT/loss proxy is the honest choice.
121	//
122	// Concurrency mirrors ActiveBackup: every field is guarded by s.mu; the send path
123	// recomputes under the lock and never calls back into the Bind, so a Bind holding
124	// its own send lock across Pick cannot deadlock.
125	//
126	// Statefulness and the T40 nudge. Pick is STATEFUL — it advances the offered-load
127	// meter, the aggregation gate, the pacing buckets, and the smooth-weighted-round-
128	// robin credits, so ONE Pick == ONE frame. The eager-failover nudge (defect D18)
129	// must therefore NOT call Pick (it would steal a distribution slot and skew the
130	// weights); it calls Recompute, which refreshes only the liveness-derived eligible/
131	// active set and logs any active-path transition, touching NONE of that per-frame
132	// distribution state.
133	type WeightedScheduler struct {
134		health  []PathHealth  // index = priority; [0] is the preferred primary
135		quality []PathQuality // parallel to health; element may be nil (neutral weight)
136		clock   telemetry.Clock
137		cfg     WeightedConfig
138		log     log.Logger
139		fec     FECPolicy // P3+ redundancy seam; nil = no FEC
140	
141		mu sync.Mutex
142	
143		// Offered-load meter: an exponentially-weighted event-rate estimator (each Pick is
144		// one event; the estimate converges to the offered Pick rate in events/sec).
145		loadRate float64
146		haveLoad bool
147		lastLoad time.Time
148	
149		// Aggregation gate (hysteresis).
150		aggregating bool
151		belowSince  time.Time // when load first fell below the disengage threshold; zero == not pending
152	
153		// Smooth-weighted-round-robin credits, parallel to health (global path index).
154		current []float64
155	
156		// Per-path pacing token buckets, parallel to health.
157		tokens   []float64
158		lastFill time.Time
159		haveFill bool
160	
161		// Active-primary cache: the best eligible path, for eager-failover transition
162		// logging (parity with ActiveBackup). -1 == none.
163		active int
164	
165		// Pacer-shedding log rate limiter: under sustained overload shedding is per-frame
166		// (thousands/s), so the "pacer shedding" diagnostic is coalesced to at most one
167		// record per shedLogInterval, carrying the count shed since the last record.
168		shedCount   int
169		lastShedLog time.Time
170	}
171	
172	// shedLogInterval bounds how often the pacer-shedding diagnostic is emitted, so a
173	// sustained overload logs a periodic coalesced record rather than one line per
174	// dropped frame.
175	const shedLogInterval = 1 * time.Second
176	
177	// compile-time proof WeightedScheduler is a (dynamic) Scheduler.
178	var (
179		_ Scheduler        = (*WeightedScheduler)(nil)
180		_ DynamicScheduler = (*WeightedScheduler)(nil)
181	)
182	
183	// NewWeighted builds the weighted-aggregation scheduler over parallel health and
184	// quality slices in priority order (index 0 = preferred primary). health and quality
185	// must be the same length; a quality element MAY be nil (that path then gets a
186	// neutral weight until a real PathQuality is wired). It fails fast on an empty or
187	// length-mismatched set, a nil health element, a nil dependency, or an invalid config.
188	func NewWeighted(health []PathHealth, quality []PathQuality, cfg WeightedConfig, clock telemetry.Clock, logger log.Logger) (*WeightedScheduler, error) {
189		if len(health) == 0 {
190			return nil, fmt.Errorf("sched: at least one path health source is required")
191		}
192		if len(quality) != len(health) {
193			return nil, fmt.Errorf("sched: quality set (%d) must be one entry per health source (%d)", len(quality), len(health))
194		}
195		for i, h := range health {
196			if h == nil {
197				return nil, fmt.Errorf("sched: path %d health source is nil", i)
198			}
199		}
200		if clock == nil {
201			return nil, fmt.Errorf("sched: clock is required")
202		}
203		if logger == nil {
204			return nil, fmt.Errorf("sched: logger is required")
205		}
206		if err := cfg.validate(); err != nil {
207			return nil, err
208		}
209		return &WeightedScheduler{
210			health:  append([]PathHealth(nil), health...),
211			quality: append([]PathQuality(nil), quality...),
212			clock:   clock,
213			cfg:     cfg,
214			log:     logger.Component("sched"),
215			current: make([]float64, len(health)),
216			tokens:  fullBuckets(len(health), cfg.PacingBurst),
217			active:  -1,
218		}, nil
219	}
220	
221	// SetFEC installs the P3+ redundancy policy (nil clears it). It is the documented
222	// extension point for FEC-aware path selection; the current single-path Pick does
223	// not consult it (redundant transmission needs the P3 Bind fan-out), but
224	// RedundantPaths exposes it so a P3 Bind can query the additional paths per frame.
225	func (s *WeightedScheduler) SetFEC(fec FECPolicy) {
226		s.mu.Lock()
227		defer s.mu.Unlock()
228		s.fec = fec
229	}
230	
231	// RedundantPaths reports the ADDITIONAL path indices a frame scheduled onto chosen
232	// should also egress on, per the installed FECPolicy over the current eligible set.
233	// It returns nil when no policy is installed (no FEC). It is the P3 hook a redundant-
234	// transmission Bind would call alongside Pick; today it is a documented, unit-tested
235	// seam with no in-tree caller on the hot path.
236	func (s *WeightedScheduler) RedundantPaths(chosen int) []int {
237		s.mu.Lock()
238		defer s.mu.Unlock()
239		if s.fec == nil {
240			return nil
241		}
242		return s.fec.RedundantPaths(chosen, s.eligibleLocked())
243	}
244	
245	// Pick selects the path for the NEXT frame and advances all per-frame distribution
246	// state (offered-load meter, aggregation gate, pacing buckets, round-robin credits).
247	// It returns a negative value when no path is eligible OR when every eligible path
248	// is paced out (the frame is dropped rather than queued — pacing bounds egress and,
249	// by dropping the overflow, bounds the send backlog). It is safe for concurrent
250	// callers.
251	func (s *WeightedScheduler) Pick(class FrameClass) int {
252		now := s.clock.Now()
253		s.mu.Lock()
254		defer s.mu.Unlock()
255	
256		// Capture the inter-sample idle gap BEFORE observeLoadLocked advances lastLoad: a
257		// long gap since the previous offered frame is itself the strongest evidence of low
258		// load, and the collapse hysteresis must count it (criticism #1 — otherwise an
259		// abrupt overload stop leaves the gate engaged across the whole idle span and the
260		// next low burst dribbles onto the metered path).
261		gap := s.loadGapLocked(now)
262		s.recomputeLocked(now)       // liveness refresh + eager-failover log
263		s.observeLoadLocked(now)     // this Pick is one offered frame (decays across the gap)
264		s.updateGateLocked(now, gap) // engage/disengage aggregation (hysteresis, idle-aware)
265		s.refillLocked(now)          // top up pacing buckets
266	
267		eligible := s.eligibleLocked()
268		if len(eligible) == 0 {
269			return PickNone
270		}
271		if !s.aggregating {
272			// Data-thrift: at low load only the primary (best eligible) carries traffic;
273			// the metered backup stays idle. A path-down excludes it from eligible, so
274			// eligible[0] is the surviving highest-priority path — failover is preserved.
275			return s.serveLocked(now, eligible[0], class)
276		}
277		weights := s.weightsLocked(eligible)
278		return s.selectAggregatingLocked(now, eligible, weights, class)
279	}
280	
281	// loadGapLocked returns the wall-clock gap since the previous offered-load sample, or
282	// 0 before the first sample. Caller holds s.mu.
283	func (s *WeightedScheduler) loadGapLocked(now time.Time) time.Duration {
284		if !s.haveLoad {
285			return 0
286		}
287		gap := now.Sub(s.lastLoad)
288		if gap < 0 {
289			gap = 0
290		}
291		return gap
292	}
293	
294	// Recompute refreshes only the liveness-derived eligible/active set from the current
295	// PathHealth and logs any active-path transition. It advances NONE of the per-frame
296	// distribution state (load meter, gate, pacing, round-robin credits), so the eager-
297	// failover nudge (defect D18/T40) drives an egress-lull failover recompute through it
298	// WITHOUT stealing a weighted-distribution slot the way a spurious Pick would. It is
299	// safe for concurrent callers.
300	func (s *WeightedScheduler) Recompute() {
301		now := s.clock.Now()
302		s.mu.Lock()
303		defer s.mu.Unlock()
304		s.recomputeLocked(now)
305	}
306	
307	// recomputeLocked refreshes the active-primary cache (best eligible path) and logs a
308	// transition on change. Caller holds s.mu.
309	func (s *WeightedScheduler) recomputeLocked(_ time.Time) {
310		best := s.bestEligibleLocked()
311		s.setActiveLocked(best)
312	}
313	
314	// serveLocked applies pacing to a single chosen path: it returns idx when the frame
315	// is admitted, or PickPaced when pacing is on and the path has no token (the frame is
316	// shed — dropped, bounding egress and backlog — and the shedding diagnostic is
317	// rate-limited). A ClassControl frame is pacing-EXEMPT (defect D22): it egresses on
318	// idx unconditionally, spending no token and never shed, so bulk overload cannot
319	// starve WireGuard rekey. Caller holds s.mu.
320	func (s *WeightedScheduler) serveLocked(now time.Time, idx int, class FrameClass) int {
321		if class == ClassControl {
322			return idx
323		}
324		if s.tryConsumeLocked(idx) {
325			return idx
326		}
327		return s.shedLocked(now)
328	}
329	
330	// selectAggregatingLocked picks a path proportional to weight (smooth weighted round
331	// robin) and applies pacing. When the round-robin's ideal pick is paced out it falls
332	// through to any other eligible path that still has a token, so aggregate egress is
333	// bounded by the SUM of the per-path paces; when every eligible path is paced out it
334	// sheds the frame (PickPaced). A ClassControl frame is pacing-EXEMPT (defect D22): it
335	// egresses on the weight-ideal path spending no token and is never shed, so bulk
336	// overload across every path cannot starve WireGuard rekey. Caller holds s.mu.
337	func (s *WeightedScheduler) selectAggregatingLocked(now time.Time, eligible []int, weights []float64, class FrameClass) int {
338		ideal := s.swrrPickLocked(eligible, weights)
339		if class == ClassControl {
340			return ideal
341		}
342		if s.tryConsumeLocked(ideal) {
343			return ideal
344		}
345		for _, gi := range eligible {
346			if gi == ideal {
347				continue
348			}
349			if s.tryConsumeLocked(gi) {
350				return gi
351			}
352		}
353		return s.shedLocked(now)
354	}
355	
356	// shedLocked records one paced-out (shed) frame and emits the coalesced "pacer
357	// shedding" diagnostic at most once per shedLogInterval. It returns PickPaced so the
358	// Send path can map the drop to a distinct, non-outage error/log (criticism #3): the
359	// paths are healthy, this is deliberate rate limiting. Caller holds s.mu.
360	func (s *WeightedScheduler) shedLocked(now time.Time) int {
361		s.shedCount++
362		if s.lastShedLog.IsZero() || now.Sub(s.lastShedLog) >= shedLogInterval {
363			s.log.Info("scheduler pacer shedding",
364				"shed_frames", s.shedCount,
365				"load_fps", s.loadRate,
366			)
367			s.lastShedLog = now
368			s.shedCount = 0
369		}
370		return PickPaced
371	}
372	
373	// swrrPickLocked is nginx's smooth weighted round-robin over the eligible set: it
374	// advances each eligible path's credit by its weight, picks the max-credit path, and
375	// subtracts the total weight from the winner. Over many calls the selection frequency
376	// of each path converges to its weight share, interleaved smoothly rather than
377	// bursted. Caller holds s.mu; weights is aligned to eligible order.
378	func (s *WeightedScheduler) swrrPickLocked(eligible []int, weights []float64) int {
379		var total float64
380		for _, w := range weights {
381			total += w
382		}
383		best := -1
384		var bestCur float64
385		for k, gi := range eligible {
386			s.current[gi] += weights[k]
387			if best < 0 || s.current[gi] > bestCur {
388				best = gi
389				bestCur = s.current[gi]
390			}
391		}
392		s.current[best] -= total
393		return best
394	}
395	
396	// weightsLocked computes the normalized Mathis-style weight for each eligible path
397	// from its PathQuality Estimate (RTT/loss). A path with NO usable quality — a nil
398	// quality source, or an all-zero Estimate (RTT==0: no probe sample yet) — is treated
399	// as genuinely NEUTRAL: it receives the MEAN of the measured paths' weights, so an
400	// unwired/cold path splits evenly rather than dominating. This matters because the
401	// floors would otherwise hand an all-zero Estimate the MAXIMUM weight (RTT floored to
402	// WeightRTTFloor, zero loss), letting a health-only path admitted via AddPath siphon
403	// the dominant share — the opposite of neutral. When every eligible path is neutral,
404	// all get equal weight. Caller holds s.mu; the result is aligned to eligible order and
405	// sums to 1.
406	func (s *WeightedScheduler) weightsLocked(eligible []int) []float64 {
407		w := make([]float64, len(eligible))
408		measured := make([]bool, len(eligible))
409		rttFloor := float64(s.cfg.WeightRTTFloor)
410		var sumMeasured float64
411		nMeasured := 0
412		for k, gi := range eligible {
413			q := s.quality[gi]
414			if q == nil {
415				continue // unwired: neutral, filled below
416			}
417			est := q.Estimate()
418			if est.RTT <= 0 {
419				continue // no RTT sample yet (cold estimator): neutral, filled below
420			}
421			rtt := float64(est.RTT)
422			if rtt < rttFloor {
423				rtt = rttFloor
424			}
425			loss := est.Loss
426			if loss < 0 {
427				loss = 0
428			}
429			wi := 1.0 / (rtt * math.Sqrt(loss+s.cfg.WeightLossFloor))
430			w[k] = wi
431			measured[k] = true
432			sumMeasured += wi
433			nMeasured++
434		}
435		// Neutral weight: the mean of the measured weights (so a neutral path splits evenly
436		// with the measured set, never outweighing it); equal weights when nothing measured.
437		neutral := 1.0
438		if nMeasured > 0 {
439			neutral = sumMeasured / float64(nMeasured)
440		}
441		var sum float64
442		for k := range w {
443			if !measured[k] {
444				w[k] = neutral
445			}
446			sum += w[k]
447		}
448		if sum <= 0 {
449			for k := range w {
450				w[k] = 1.0 / float64(len(w))
451			}
452			return w
453		}
454		for k := range w {
455			w[k] /= sum
456		}
457		return w
458	}
459	
460	// observeLoadLocked folds one offered frame into the exponentially-weighted event-
461	// rate estimator. Each event adds 1/tau (per-second units) after decaying the prior
462	// estimate by exp(-dt/tau); the steady-state estimate converges to the offered rate
463	// in events/sec. It is robust to dt==0 (many Picks at one clock instant just add
464	// without decay). Caller holds s.mu.
465	func (s *WeightedScheduler) observeLoadLocked(now time.Time) {
466		tau := s.cfg.LoadTau.Seconds()
467		if !s.haveLoad {
468			s.haveLoad = true
469			s.lastLoad = now
470			s.loadRate = 0
471		} else {
472			dt := now.Sub(s.lastLoad).Seconds()
473			if dt < 0 {
474				dt = 0
475			}
476			s.loadRate *= math.Exp(-dt / tau)
477			s.lastLoad = now
478		}
479		s.loadRate += 1.0 / tau
480	}
481	
482	// updateGateLocked engages or disengages aggregation from the smoothed offered load,
483	// with hysteresis: engage the instant load exceeds EngageFraction*capacity; collapse
484	// back to primary-only only after load stays below DisengageFraction*capacity for
485	// CollapseDwell. The two-threshold band plus the dwell keep the metered path from
486	// flapping on/off around a single point (requirement 2). gap is the wall-clock idle
487	// span since the previous offered frame.
488	//
489	// Idle time IS low load (criticism #1). The gate only advances on a Pick, so an
490	// overload that stops ABRUPTLY (load still above disengage on the last frame) would
491	// otherwise stay engaged across an arbitrarily long idle span and stripe the first
492	// ~CollapseDwell of the NEXT low burst onto the metered backup — a data-thrift leak.
493	// Two provisions close it: (a) an idle gap that alone reaches CollapseDwell forces an
494	// immediate collapse, since a gap that long is itself proof of sustained low load; and
495	// (b) belowSince is BACKDATED to when load actually dropped (the previous sample,
496	// now-gap) rather than seeded at this post-idle frame, so the dwell counts the idle.
497	// The load EWMA decays across gap in observeLoadLocked, so a post-idle frame reads
498	// ~0 and the collapse fires on the FIRST frame of the next burst. Caller holds s.mu.
499	func (s *WeightedScheduler) updateGateLocked(now time.Time, gap time.Duration) {
500		engage := s.cfg.EngageFraction * s.cfg.PerPathCapacity
501		disengage := s.cfg.DisengageFraction * s.cfg.PerPathCapacity
502		if !s.aggregating {
503			if s.loadRate > engage {
504				s.aggregating = true
505				s.belowSince = time.Time{}
506				s.log.Info("scheduler aggregation change", "to", "aggregating", "load_fps", s.loadRate)
507			}
508			return
509		}
510		// (a) A single idle gap of at least the dwell is conclusive: collapse now.
511		if gap >= s.cfg.CollapseDwell {
512			s.aggregating = false
513			s.belowSince = time.Time{}
514			s.log.Info("scheduler aggregation change", "to", "collapsed", "reason", "idle gap", "gap", gap.String())
515			return
516		}
517		if s.loadRate < disengage {
518			if s.belowSince.IsZero() {
519				// (b) Backdate to when load dropped: the previous sample (now-gap), not this
520				// post-idle frame, so the idle span counts toward the dwell.
521				s.belowSince = now.Add(-gap)
522			}
523			if now.Sub(s.belowSince) >= s.cfg.CollapseDwell {
524				s.aggregating = false
525				s.belowSince = time.Time{}
526				s.log.Info("scheduler aggregation change", "to", "collapsed", "reason", "sustained low load", "load_fps", s.loadRate)
527			}
528			return
529		}
530		// Load rose back above the disengage threshold: reset the collapse dwell.
531		s.belowSince = time.Time{}
532	}
533	
534	// refillLocked tops up every per-path token bucket by PerPathCapacity·elapsed, capped
535	// at PacingBurst. The first call seeds every bucket full. It is a no-op accountant
536	// when pacing is disabled (tryConsumeLocked always admits), but the buckets are still
537	// maintained so toggling pacing needs no re-seed. Caller holds s.mu.
538	func (s *WeightedScheduler) refillLocked(now time.Time) {
539		if !s.haveFill {
540			s.haveFill = true
541			s.lastFill = now
542			for i := range s.tokens {
543				s.tokens[i] = s.cfg.PacingBurst
544			}
545			return
546		}
547		dt := now.Sub(s.lastFill).Seconds()
548		if dt < 0 {
549			dt = 0
550		}
551		add := s.cfg.PerPathCapacity * dt
552		for i := range s.tokens {
553			s.tokens[i] += add
554			if s.tokens[i] > s.cfg.PacingBurst {
555				s.tokens[i] = s.cfg.PacingBurst
556			}
557		}
558		s.lastFill = now
559	}
560	
561	// tryConsumeLocked spends one token from path idx's bucket, reporting whether the
562	// frame is admitted. With pacing disabled it always admits (the buckets are inert).
563	// Caller holds s.mu.
564	func (s *WeightedScheduler) tryConsumeLocked(idx int) bool {
565		if !s.cfg.Pacing {
566			return true
567		}
568		if s.tokens[idx] >= 1 {
569			s.tokens[idx]--
570			return true
571		}
572		return false
573	}
574	
575	// eligibleLocked returns the global indices of paths reporting StateUp, in priority
576	// order. Caller holds s.mu.
577	func (s *WeightedScheduler) eligibleLocked() []int {
578		e := make([]int, 0, len(s.health))
579		for i := range s.health {
580			if s.health[i].State() == telemetry.StateUp {
581				e = append(e, i)
582			}
583		}
584		return e
585	}
586	
587	// bestEligibleLocked returns the lowest-index path reporting StateUp, or -1.
588	func (s *WeightedScheduler) bestEligibleLocked() int {
589		for i := range s.health {
590			if s.health[i].State() == telemetry.StateUp {
591				return i
592			}
593		}
594		return -1
595	}
596	
597	// setActiveLocked stores the active-primary index and logs a transition only when it
598	// changes, so a saturated send path does not log on every Pick. The message matches
599	// ActiveBackup's ("scheduler active path change") so downstream log parsing (the e2e
600	// failover harness) reads either policy identically.
601	func (s *WeightedScheduler) setActiveLocked(idx int) {
602		if s.active == idx {
603			return
604		}
605		from := s.active
606		s.active = idx
607		s.log.Info("scheduler active path change", "from", from, "to", idx, "reason", "weighted recompute")
608	}
609	
610	// AddPath admits h as a new lowest-priority path and returns its index (the new
611	// tail). Its quality source is h itself when h also satisfies PathQuality (the
612	// production *Prober does); a health-ONLY source (no PathQuality) is recorded with a
613	// nil quality and weightsLocked then gives it the NEUTRAL (mean) weight, so it splits
614	// evenly and never siphons the dominant share. Appending at the tail leaves every
615	// existing index unchanged and the new path only carries traffic once aggregation
616	// engages, so a runtime admission never disturbs the surviving paths' distribution
617	// (T30). It is safe for concurrent callers.
618	func (s *WeightedScheduler) AddPath(h PathHealth) (int, error) {
619		if h == nil {
620			return 0, fmt.Errorf("sched: cannot add a nil path health source")
621		}
622		s.mu.Lock()
623		defer s.mu.Unlock()
624		s.health = append(s.health, h)
625		s.quality = append(s.quality, asQuality(h))
626		s.current = append(s.current, 0)
627		s.tokens = append(s.tokens, s.cfg.PacingBurst)
628		return len(s.health) - 1, nil
629	}
630	
631	// RemovePath drops the path at index i (shifting higher indices down by one) and
632	// clears the cached active selection so the next Pick/Recompute re-derives it from
633	// live liveness. The round-robin credits are positional and compacted with the path
634	// list; a brief re-warm of the smoothing after a membership change is harmless. It is
635	// safe for concurrent callers.
636	func (s *WeightedScheduler) RemovePath(i int) error {
637		s.mu.Lock()
638		defer s.mu.Unlock()
639		if i < 0 || i >= len(s.health) {
640			return fmt.Errorf("sched: remove path index %d out of range [0,%d)", i, len(s.health))
641		}
642		s.health = append(s.health[:i], s.health[i+1:]...)
643		s.quality = append(s.quality[:i], s.quality[i+1:]...)
644		s.current = append(s.current[:i], s.current[i+1:]...)
645		s.tokens = append(s.tokens[:i], s.tokens[i+1:]...)
646		s.active = -1
647		return nil
648	}
649	
650	// SetPaths replaces the entire health list (priority order, index 0 = preferred
651	// primary), rebuilding the parallel quality slice from the new sources, resetting the
652	// round-robin credits and pacing buckets, and collapsing aggregation to primary-only
653	// so the next Pick re-derives everything from live liveness. The Bind calls it on
654	// every Open to re-align the scheduler with the freshly-rebuilt path slice (T30). It
655	// fails fast on an empty set or a nil element, matching NewWeighted. It is safe for
656	// concurrent callers.
657	func (s *WeightedScheduler) SetPaths(health []PathHealth) error {
658		if len(health) == 0 {
659			return fmt.Errorf("sched: at least one path health source is required")
660		}
661		for i, h := range health {
662			if h == nil {
663				return fmt.Errorf("sched: path %d health source is nil", i)
664			}
665		}
666		s.mu.Lock()
667		defer s.mu.Unlock()
668		s.health = append([]PathHealth(nil), health...)
669		s.quality = make([]PathQuality, len(health))
670		for i, h := range health {
671			s.quality[i] = asQuality(h)
672		}
673		s.current = make([]float64, len(health))
674		s.tokens = fullBuckets(len(health), s.cfg.PacingBurst)
675		s.haveFill = false
676		s.aggregating = false
677		s.belowSince = time.Time{}
678		s.active = -1
679		return nil
680	}
681	
682	// asQuality returns h as a PathQuality when it also implements that interface (the
683	// production *telemetry.Prober does — one source drives both liveness and weight), or
684	// nil so the path gets a neutral weight.
685	func asQuality(h PathHealth) PathQuality {
686		if q, ok := h.(PathQuality); ok {
687			return q
688		}
689		return nil
690	}
691	
692	// fullBuckets returns n token buckets each seeded to burst.
693	func fullBuckets(n int, burst float64) []float64 {
694		t := make([]float64, n)
695		for i := range t {
696			t[i] = burst
697		}
698		return t
699	}

25	// socketRecvBuffer is the SO_RCVBUF requested on every per-path socket, matching
26	// wireguard-go's StdNetBind (~7 MiB). A large receive buffer absorbs the bursts a
27	// bonded, reordered flow delivers while the userspace engine catches up; the
28	// pass-through P0 Bind used the OS default and lost datagrams under load (P0
29	// findings §1/§2). The kernel silently caps the request at net.core.rmem_max, so
30	// the effective size may be smaller on an untuned host — best-effort by design.
31	const socketRecvBuffer = 7 << 20
32	
33	// multipathBatchSize is the number of datagrams a ReceiveFunc / Send handles per
34	// call. T12 keeps it at 1 (one syscall per datagram, per path); GSO/GRO batching
35	// is best-effort future work (P0 findings §2) tracked separately and does not
36	// change the wire format.
37	const multipathBatchSize = 1
38	
39	// maxDatagram bounds a single received outer datagram. It comfortably exceeds a
40	// full-MTU inner packet plus every outer/WG/amnezia-junk overhead.
41	const maxDatagram = 65535
42	
43	// Receive-resequencer tuning (T18), sized against P0 findings §6.
44	//
45	// resequencerWindow is the maximum span of outer-seq positions the receive
46	// resequencer buffers while it waits for an out-of-order frame. WG's inner RFC
47	// 6479 anti-replay window is 8128 messages (docs/p0-findings.md §6); the window
48	// here sits ~4x below that, so even a worst-case burst of reordered/held frames
49	// stays comfortably inside the inner filter's tolerance while spanning far more
50	// packets than the measured ~19 ms emulated cross-path skew (and the larger,
51	// more variable real Starlink/5G skew) needs at realistic per-path rates.
52	//
53	// resequencerTimeout bounds how long a head-of-line-blocked run is held for a
54	// missing (presumed-lost) lower frame before that gap is skipped and the run
55	// released. It caps the added latency — and the stall a slow/lossy path can
56	// impose on the whole bond (P0 §7 head-of-line concern) — to a few multiples of
57	// a Starlink RTT (~45 ms) rather than holding a gap forever.
58	const (
59		resequencerWindow  = 2048
60		resequencerTimeout = 250 * time.Millisecond
61	)
62	
63	// defaultMaxDemuxSources caps the source->peer demux map (peerBySource), the
64	// PROVISIONAL/unbound-source tracking state whose growth an attacker probes at
65	// bootstrap (Q26/Q27). It is sized SEPARATELY from the steady-state peer set
66	// (m.peersByName, bounded by the static configured [[wireguard.peers]]): a source
67	// AddrPort enters this map only on an authenticated PROBE (T88). The map is keyed by
68	// the full source netip.AddrPort (address+port, D47) — NOT the bare address — so two
69	// peers behind ONE public IP (CGNAT) bind to distinct entries and demux independently.
70	//
71	// This GLOBAL cap is the outer bound; within it a PER-PEER quota (maxDemuxSources /
72	// len(peers), floor 1, D49) is enforced so one party holding ONE valid psk that floods
73	// spoofed sources to its OWN peer exhausts only ITS OWN quota and never starves another
74	// peer's bootstrap PROBE (Q27(1) cross-peer isolation). Two eviction/drop regimes apply
75	// to a NEW source AddrPort:
76	//   - SAME-peer (roam port-churn): a peer already at its per-peer quota that authenticates
77	//     a NEW AddrPort for ITSELF evicts its OWN oldest binding (LRU within the peer) to admit
78	//     it — a live roaming peer is NEVER dropped and its footprint never grows past quota, so
79	//     it can never evict ANOTHER peer's slot (never-evict-live w.r.t. others holds).
80	//   - CROSS-peer (drop-on-exhaustion): a peer BELOW its quota whose NEW source would grow the
81	//     map past the GLOBAL cap is refused — it may not steal another peer's headroom. Bootstrap
82	//     degrades; WG retransmits re-drive it once a slot frees.
83	//
84	// An already-bound (live) source is NEVER evicted by another peer. Roam/re-affirm of an
85	// already-present AddrPort (T90) does not grow the map, so it is never blocked by the cap. A
86	// dead peer's bindings are reclaimed on teardown (TearDownPeer), freeing slots. The default is
87	// generous relative to any realistic concentrator's peer×roam-churn count yet bounds the flood
88	// surface.
89	const defaultMaxDemuxSources = 1024
90	
91	var (
92		// ErrNoHealthyPath is exported (I4) so the device-package engineLogger adapter can
93		// errors.Is-match it against the engine's wrapped Errorf args to gate the startup
94		// no-healthy-path warmup coalescing without string-matching the log message.
95		ErrNoHealthyPath = errors.New("bind: no healthy path with a known remote endpoint")
96		// errPacerShedding is returned by Send when the scheduler shed the datagram for
97		// pacing (PickPaced) while paths are healthy — deliberate rate limiting, NOT an
98		// outage. It is DISTINCT from ErrNoHealthyPath so operator logs and the e2e
99		// log-grep harness can tell shedding from total path failure (the drop behavior is
100		// identical to the pre-existing no-path case; only the diagnostic differs). The
101		// coalesced, rate-limited "pacer shedding" record is emitted by the scheduler.
102		errPacerShedding = errors.New("bind: datagram shed by send pacer (paths healthy, rate limited)")
103		errClosed        = net.ErrClosed
104	)

106	// sharedPathState is the per-SOCKET state of one configured uplink, SHARED across
107	// every peer bound beneath the Bind: its stable path-id, source address, and the
108	// source-bound UDP socket (drained by exactly one Bind-owned readLoop). A concentrator
109	// front-ends many peers on the SAME socket, so this identity is owned once per socket
110	// and referenced by every peer's per-(peer,path) view (peerPathState). On the single-peer
111	// edge/hub there is exactly one peer, so each shared path has exactly one peerPathState.
112	// The deferred-path machinery (deferredPath, m.deferred) likewise operates on this shared
113	// socket layer — a runtime add/remove of a shared path fans the per-(peer,path) state out
114	// to every bound peer (see attachSharedPathLocked / RemovePath).
115	type sharedPathState struct {
116		name string
117		id   uint8
118		src  netip.Addr
119		conn *net.UDPConn
120	
121		// views is the per-peer VIEW set of THIS shared socket — one peerPathState per bound
122		// peer — published copy-on-write through an atomic.Pointer so the single Bind-owned
123		// readLoop demuxes an inbound datagram to its owning peer's view WITHOUT m.mu on the
124		// receive hot path (the same lock-free-publish discipline peersView/resequencer use).
125		// It is (re)published under m.mu at every fan-out site (Open / attachPeerPathLocked /
126		// deferred promote, and each concentrator peer bind). On the single-peer edge/hub it
127		// holds exactly one entry (the primary's view), which handleInbound reads as "no demux
128		// needed" — the byte-identical fast path. len(views)>1 marks a shared concentrator
129		// socket whose datagrams must be source-demuxed to the owning peer (T88).
130		views atomic.Pointer[[]*peerPathState]
131	}
132	
133	// addViewLocked publishes pp as a per-peer view of this shared socket for the lock-free
134	// receive demux (T88). Copy-on-write: it republishes a NEW slice with pp appended, so a
135	// readLoop mid-iteration over the previously-published snapshot is never disturbed. The
136	// caller holds m.mu (every fan-out site does), which serializes writers; readers Load
137	// without a lock.
138	func (sp *sharedPathState) addViewLocked(pp *peerPathState) {
139		old := sp.views.Load()
140		var next []*peerPathState
141		if old != nil {
142			next = make([]*peerPathState, len(*old), len(*old)+1)
143			copy(next, *old)
144		}
145		next = append(next, pp)
146		sp.views.Store(&next)
147	}

149	// peerPathState is one peer's per-(peer,path) VIEW of a shared uplink: that peer's own
150	// decode Codec (each path receives on its own goroutine, so the Codec's scratch is never
151	// shared), the peer's own learned/configured return remote, the peer's own probe
152	// initiator for this path, and the peer's own per-path OUTER-wire byte counters. The
153	// remote is either configured (edge dest_addr / peer endpoint) or LEARNED from inbound
154	// traffic (concentrator) — but it lives strictly BELOW the engine's single virtual
155	// endpoint, so the engine never sees this per-(peer,path) bookkeeping churn.
156	//
157	// It EMBEDS its *sharedPathState so the socket identity (name/id/src/conn) is reached
158	// transparently, and back-references the owning peerState so a receive handler resolves
159	// exactly that peer's resequencer/reflector/FEC decoder.
160	type peerPathState struct {
161		*sharedPathState
162		peer  *peerState
163		codec *frame.Codec
164		// prober is this (peer,path)'s own probe initiator (nil when the bind runs without the
165		// probe transport). It is set at path creation and immutable for the path's life,
166		// so the Bind-owned receive goroutine reaches it via the peerPathState it already
167		// holds — NOT through a shared, dynamically-mutated probers slice — which is
168		// what keeps echo handling race-free while paths are added/removed at runtime (T30).
169		prober *telemetry.Prober
170	
171		// txBytes/rxBytes are cumulative OUTER-wire byte counters for this (peer,path), the
172		// per-path traffic accounting the /metrics exposition reports (T23). Both are
173		// TRUE-WIRE-VOLUME counters: txBytes counts every outer datagram this path actually
174		// writes to its socket — DATA/PARITY on the Send hot path (T23/T24), PROBE frames
175		// emitted by emitProbes, and PROBE echoes reflected back by dispatchInbound — each
176		// counted only once the write returns a nil error; rxBytes counts every outer
177		// datagram this path's readLoop receives (DATA, PROBE, and echo alike). Neither
178		// counter is DATA-only or Send-only (D48): a healthy idle standby path still emits
179		// and echoes probes, so its txBytes keeps advancing even while active-backup
180		// collapses all DATA onto the primary. They are atomics so the send/receive/probe
181		// hot paths increment them WITHOUT taking m.mu (lock-free) from whichever goroutine
182		// performs the write (Send's caller, the probe-loop goroutine, or this path's
183		// readLoop), and the scrape/snapshot path reads them with a plain atomic Load.
184		txBytes atomic.Uint64
185		rxBytes atomic.Uint64
186	
187		mu        sync.Mutex
188		remote    netip.AddrPort
189		hasRemote bool
190	}

192	// peerState holds the per-PEER datapath state that was a process-global singleton before
193	// the multi-peer concentrator split: the SINGLE virtual endpoint the engine holds for this
194	// peer, the peer's send scheduler, its own outer-seq space, its shared send Codec, its
195	// probe reflector, its receive resequencer and FEC send/receive planes, its per-path probe
196	// initiators, and its per-(peer,path) views over the shared sockets. The single-peer
197	// edge/hub constructs EXACTLY ONE peerState (so behaviour is byte-identical to the pre-split
198	// singleton — Multipath embeds it as the primary and the datapath reaches its fields through
199	// that embed); the concentrator constructs one peerState per bound peer. resequencer and
200	// fecRecv stay atomic.Pointer, and outerSeq atomic, so the lock-free receive/send fast paths
201	// read them WITHOUT m.mu.
202	type peerState struct {
203		// name is the peer id/name, the key under which Multipath.peersByName holds this peer.
204		// Empty on the single-peer edge/hub (there is only one peer to key).
205		name string
206	
207		// psk is THIS peer's effective pre-shared key: the sole seam from which every frame
208		// Codec this peer derives (its sendCodec and each per-(peer,path) receive codec, via
209		// newCodec) and its probe Reflector authenticate. The single-peer edge/hub sets it to
210		// the one configured psk (so behaviour is byte-identical to the pre-split singleton);
211		// the concentrator sets a DIFFERENT psk per bound peer, so one peer's codec/reflector
212		// rejects another peer's frames (T84). NewCodec/NewReflector PSK-derivation itself is
213		// unchanged — only WHICH psk each peer feeds them differs.
214		psk config.Key
215	
216		// Immutable per-peer collaborators, built at construction and persisting across the
217		// Open→Close socket lifecycle (the concentrator pins virt's destination once for the
218		// process life, so virt in particular must NOT be recreated per Open).
219		virt      *udpEndpoint
220		scheduler sched.Scheduler
221		reflector *telemetry.Reflector
222		// newProber mints a prober for a path admitted to THIS peer at runtime (T30); nil
223		// disables runtime path addition for the peer (a bind without the probe transport).
224		newProber ProberFactory
225		// probers is this peer's BOOT-TIME per-path probe initiator set, in durable-membership
226		// (m.defs) order — bound AND deferred — shared with this peer's scheduler. At Open each
227		// bound entry is bound onto its peerPathState (pp.prober), and thereafter the hot paths
228		// reach a path's prober through the peerPathState — never by indexing this slice — so a
229		// runtime path add/remove cannot race echo handling. Nil when the peer runs without the
230		// probe transport; a nil here also gates the whole probe loop off.
231		probers []*telemetry.Prober
232	
233		// Per-Open state, (re)built by Open and cleared by Close.
234		sendCodec *frame.Codec
235		paths     []*peerPathState
236		outerSeq  atomic.Uint64
237		// resequencer is this peer's shared T18 receive resequencing buffer. Published
238		// atomically so the per-path readLoop goroutines read it WITHOUT m.mu.
239		resequencer atomic.Pointer[reseq.Resequencer]
240		// FEC datapath (T24), per peer. Both published atomically like resequencer so the
241		// lock-free receive fast path Loads fecRecv, and the lazy re-instantiation on re-bind
242		// (ensurePeerReceiveInstantiated, on a readLoop that must not take m.mu) can Store
243		// fecSend without racing Send's under-m.mu Load. The ENCODER inside fecSend is still
244		// mutated (Admit/Tick) only under m.mu — the atomic pointer governs publication of the
245		// fecSender object, not its encoder's single-writer discipline. Both nil when FEC is off.
246		fecSend atomic.Pointer[fecSender]
247		fecRecv atomic.Pointer[fecReceiver]
248	
249		// lifecycleMu serializes lazy (re)instantiation of the heavy trio (resequencer, fecRecv,
250		// fecSend) on a readLoop goroutine (ensurePeerReceiveInstantiated) against teardown's
251		// clearing of that trio (teardownPeerLocked), so a teardown interleaving mid-instantiation
252		// can never leave a half-published plane (a fecRecv/fecSend without its resequencer) nor
253		// resurrect a plane on a torn-down peer that the next re-bind then reuses stale. It is a
254		// LEAF lock taken either ALONE (instantiation — which must never take m.mu, so Close's
255		// readersWG.Wait under m.mu cannot deadlock on it) or UNDER m.mu (teardown), giving the
256		// single fixed order m.mu -> lifecycleMu and no cycle (instantiation never reaches for m.mu).
257		lifecycleMu sync.Mutex
258	}

260	// newPeerState builds the durable per-peer datapath state whose probe Reflector — and,
261	// through ps.psk, every frame Codec this peer later derives in Open/AddPath (its sendCodec
262	// and each per-(peer,path) receive codec, via newCodec) — authenticate under THIS peer's
263	// psk. It is the seam that replaces the pre-split single-psk assumption (T84): the
264	// single-peer edge/hub mints exactly one (the primary) from the sole configured psk, while
265	// the concentrator mints one per bound peer from that peer's DIFFERENT effective psk, so a
266	// peerState built from a different psk gets its own codec/reflector and cross-psk frames are
267	// rejected. virt is created here (not per Open) because the concentrator pins its destination
268	// once for the process life. The Reflector draws its per-path challenges from crypto/rand,
269	// exactly as before. The per-Open fields (sendCodec, paths, resequencer, FEC planes) are
270	// left zero for Open to (re)build.
271	func newPeerState(name string, psk config.Key, scheduler sched.Scheduler, newProber ProberFactory, probers []*telemetry.Prober) *peerState {
272		return &peerState{
273			name:      name,
274			psk:       psk,
275			virt:      &udpEndpoint{},
276			scheduler: scheduler,
277			reflector: telemetry.NewReflector(psk, rand.Reader),
278			newProber: newProber,
279			probers:   probers,
280		}
281	}

283	// newCodec derives a fresh frame Codec bound to THIS peer's psk — the send Codec at Open and
284	// each per-(peer,path) receive Codec share the derivation but not the instance, since a Codec
285	// is not safe for concurrent use (each receive path decodes on its own goroutine). Deriving
286	// from ps.psk (not a Multipath-wide key) is what makes a path's receive Codec the codec of the
287	// peer the path is bound to (T84).
288	func (ps *peerState) newCodec() (*frame.Codec, error) {
289		return frame.NewCodec(ps.psk)
290	}

292	// deferredPath is a configured path whose WELL-FORMED source_addr was not yet
293	// assignable at Open (net.ListenUDP -> EADDRNOTAVAIL: no interface holds the
294	// address, e.g. a 5G modem with no DHCP lease at boot). Rather than tear the whole
295	// bond down, Open records the path here instead of binding it: the tunnel comes up
296	// on the paths that DID bind, and this path stays DOWN — its prober, never fed an
297	// echo, reports StateDown, which the scheduler excludes from Pick, exactly as the
298	// runtime path-down model treats a live-but-silent path. It carries the boot-time
299	// prober so the T55 background reconcile that retries the bind reuses the SAME
300	// path-id stamp instead of minting a new one. prober is nil only on a bind without
301	// the probe transport, which never defers (see Open).
302	type deferredPath struct {
303		def    config.Path
304		prober *telemetry.Prober
305	}

307	func (ps *peerPathState) setRemote(ap netip.AddrPort) {
308		ps.mu.Lock()
309		ps.remote, ps.hasRemote = ap, true
310		ps.mu.Unlock()
311	}
312	
313	func (ps *peerPathState) getRemote() (netip.AddrPort, bool) {
314		ps.mu.Lock()
315		defer ps.mu.Unlock()
316		return ps.remote, ps.hasRemote
317	}
