319	// Multipath is the P1 bonding conn.Bind: one UDP socket per configured path
320	// (bound to the path's source address), all fronted by a SINGLE stable virtual
321	// endpoint the engine holds per peer. Send wraps each opaque WireGuard datagram
322	// in an outer DATA frame (own outer-seq + path-id) and picks a path; one
323	// Bind-owned reader per path unwraps DATA frames into the shared resequencer, and
324	// a SINGLE engine-facing ReceiveFunc drains it and hands the inner WG datagram up
325	// under the shared virtual endpoint (the fan-in that lets paths be added/removed at
326	// runtime without the engine spawning receive goroutines — T30). It replaces
327	// bind.Passthrough behind the same conn.Bind seam (device wiring is unchanged).
328	//
329	// Lifecycle: the sockets live for the duration of an Open→Close span, NOT the
330	// Multipath's whole life. The amneziawg engine calls Close() before every Open()
331	// (device.upLocked → BindUpdate → closeBindLocked, and on IpcSet listen_port /
332	// route-change events) and cycles Close↔Open on Down/Up, so — exactly like
333	// conn.StdNetBind — Open creates the per-path sockets and Close tears them down
334	// AND clears the path state so the next Open rebuilds from scratch. The "closed"
335	// state is simply "no bound sockets" (len(paths)==0); there is no separate sticky
336	// flag that a later Open would have to reset.
337	//
338	// Path policy: an injected sched.Scheduler (T15) chooses the egress path per
339	// datagram. The P1 MVP wires an active-backup scheduler (all traffic on the
340	// preferred primary, transparent failover to a backup on a T13 path-DOWN signal
341	// with failback hysteresis); the weighted/FEC-aware policy (T21) is a different
342	// Scheduler swapped in here with no Bind change. No FEC/resequencing yet
343	// (P2/P3), but every DATA frame carries its outer-seq and path-id so T18/T24 can
344	// consume them.
345	// ProberFactory mints a *telemetry.Prober for a path admitted at runtime (T30),
346	// stamped with the given stable path-id. The device wires it capturing the SAME
347	// per-boot session id, prober config, clock, PSK, and logger the boot-time probers
348	// were built with, so a runtime path's liveness is measured identically to a
349	// boot-time path (its probes join the same session so the peer's reflector adopts
350	// them without a challenge reset). It is nil on a bind built without the probe
351	// transport (the T12 unit tests), which therefore cannot add paths at runtime.
352	type ProberFactory func(name string, id uint8) *telemetry.Prober
353	
354	// sourceBinding is one entry of the source->peer demux map (peerBySource): the peer a learned
355	// source AddrPort was bound to by an authenticated PROBE, plus a monotonic insertion sequence
356	// used ONLY to choose a peer's OWN oldest binding for LRU eviction when that peer authenticates
357	// a new AddrPort for itself while already at its per-peer quota (roam port-churn, D49). The seq
358	// orders a single peer's own bindings against each other; it is never compared across peers.
359	type sourceBinding struct {
360		peer *peerState
361		seq  uint64
362	}
363	
364	type Multipath struct {
365		defs []config.Path
366	
367		// classify maps each outbound datagram to its pacer traffic class from the inner
368		// WireGuard message type, parameterized by the tunnel's Amnezia obfuscation profile
369		// so a WireGuard control frame (handshake/keepalive) is recognised — and pacing-
370		// exempted — under advanced security too, not only in vanilla mode (defect D22). It
371		// is immutable after construction and holds no lock, so Send reads it off m.mu.
372		classify wgClassifier
373	
374		// deferredListen binds a reconciled deferred path's socket (T55 background
375		// reconcile). It is an injection seam: the default pins the source IP unless dev is
376		// set (net.ListenUDP / listenPath, matching AddPath's runtime bind — I5), and a test
377		// overrides it to drive the deferred→bound transition deterministically — a
378		// source_addr "becoming assignable" — without a real interface address having to
379		// appear on the host. dev is resolveForcedDeviceBind's decision for the deferred
380		// path's resolved BindMode, computed by reconcileDeferred before the call. Immutable
381		// after construction, never nil.
382		deferredListen func(src netip.Addr, port uint16, dev string) (*net.UDPConn, error)
383	
384		// resolveDeviceBind decides AddPath's and reconcileDeferred's per-path forced-
385		// device bind (I5): whether a path's RESOLVED BindMode is config.BindModeDevice,
386		// and if so, the interface its source_addr currently resolves to (see
387		// resolveForcedDeviceBind — it has no other-path contention to check, unlike
388		// Open's planPathBinds/selectDeviceBinds). It is an injection seam mirroring
389		// deferredListen: the default is the real resolveForcedDeviceBind (a
390		// net.Interfaces() snapshot), and a test overrides it to drive a BindModeDevice
391		// path's dev deterministically without a real interface having to appear on the
392		// host (T106 round 2). Immutable after construction, never nil.
393		resolveDeviceBind func(src netip.Addr, mode config.BindMode) string
394	
395		// addPathListen binds AddPath's runtime-admitted path's socket. It is an
396		// injection seam mirroring deferredListen, scoped to AddPath's OWN call site:
397		// AddPath calls the package-level listenPath directly (it is not routed through
398		// deferredListen, which only reconcileDeferred drives), so without this seam no
399		// test can observe what dev AddPath actually threads into the bind — resolving a
400		// BindModeDevice interface via resolveDeviceBind and then discarding it at the
401		// listen call passes the full suite (T106 round 3). The default is the real
402		// listenPath; a test overrides it to capture the dev argument deterministically
403		// without a real interface having to appear on the host. Immutable after
404		// construction, never nil.
405		addPathListen func(src netip.Addr, port uint16, dev string) (*net.UDPConn, error)
406	
407		mu sync.Mutex
408	
409		// The PRIMARY peer, embedded so the single-peer datapath (Send, the receive drainer,
410		// the probe loop) and the existing single-peer tests reach its fields — virt,
411		// scheduler, reflector, newProber, probers, sendCodec, paths, outerSeq, resequencer,
412		// fecSend/fecRecv — transparently through promotion, keeping behaviour byte-identical
413		// to the pre-split singleton. It is peers[0]; the concentrator's additional peers live
414		// in peers/peersByName. The former process-global resequencer/outerSeq/scheduler now
415		// live on this peerState, NOT on Multipath.
416		*peerState
417	
418		// peers is every bound peer (len 1 on the single-peer edge/hub). The runtime shared-
419		// path add/remove fan-out iterates it so per-(peer,path) state is created/torn-down for
420		// EVERY currently-bound peer (attachSharedPathLocked / RemovePath). peersByName keys
421		// them by peer id/name.
422		peers       []*peerState
423		peersByName map[string]*peerState
424		// peersView is the LOCK-FREE snapshot of the bound peer set the single engine-facing
425		// receive drainer iterates (newReceiveFunc). m.peers is mutated only under m.mu (peer
426		// wiring / fan-out); every mutation republishes this pointer (republishPeersLocked) so
427		// the drainer enumerates EVERY bound peer's resequencer WITHOUT taking m.mu on the
428		// receive hot path — the same lock-free-publish discipline resequencer/fecRecv use. It
429		// is never nil after construction (the constructor publishes the primary-only view).
430		peersView atomic.Pointer[[]*peerState]
431		// peerByEndpoint is the inbound endpoint-keyed demux placeholder (still unused today: the
432		// single-peer edge/hub needs no demux, and the source-keyed binding below is what the
433		// concentrator receive path routes on).
434		peerByEndpoint map[netip.AddrPort]*peerState
435		// peerBySource is the inbound source-demux map: a learned source AddrPort → the peer it was
436		// bound to by an authenticated PROBE (T88). It is keyed by the full source netip.AddrPort
437		// (address+port, D47), NOT the bare address, so two peers behind ONE public IP (CGNAT, one
438		// netip.Addr, distinct ports) occupy distinct entries and demux independently. The
439		// concentrator's per-socket readLoops resolve a datagram's source to its owning peer through
440		// it; a source is bound ONLY on the first PROBE that MAC-verifies under a peer's psk (D9/D11:
441		// bindings, like remotes, are learned only from authenticated PROBEs). It is published
442		// copy-on-write through an atomic.Pointer so the receive hot path resolves a bound source with
443		// a lock-free Load (no m.mu — a reader must never block on m.mu, since Close waits on the
444		// readers WHILE holding it), and a new binding is installed lock-free by a CAS republish of a
445		// copy with the entry added (bindSourceToPeer). Nil until the first binding; the single-peer
446		// edge/hub never consults it (one peer owns every socket — handleInbound's fast path skips the
447		// demux entirely).
448		peerBySource atomic.Pointer[map[netip.AddrPort]sourceBinding]
449		// maxDemuxSources is the GLOBAL cap on peerBySource (the provisional/unbound-source demux
450		// state) so a bootstrap flood cannot grow it without bound (Q26/Q27, see defaultMaxDemuxSources).
451		// Within it bindSourceToPeer enforces a PER-PEER quota (maxDemuxSources/len(peers), floor 1,
452		// D49) for cross-peer isolation. Set once at construction and read on the lock-free bind path
453		// (bindSourceToPeer); a test may lower it to exercise cap/quota exhaustion. Zero (never set)
454		// means "no cap".
455		maxDemuxSources int
456		// bindSeq is a monotonic counter stamped on each source binding to order a peer's OWN
457		// bindings for per-peer LRU eviction (D49 roam port-churn). Read/incremented on the lock-free
458		// bind path (bindSourceToPeer) from readLoop goroutines, so it is atomic; it takes no m.mu.
459		bindSeq atomic.Uint64
460		// peerByVirt routes an OUTBOUND Send to its owning peer: the engine hands Send the
461		// single virtual endpoint (*udpEndpoint) it holds for a peer, and this map resolves
462		// that pointer to the peer's datapath state (outerSeq, scheduler, sendCodec, fecSend,
463		// per-(peer,path) set). It is the SEND-side dual of peerByEndpoint/peerBySource (which
464		// demux INBOUND datagrams). Each peer's virt is DISTINCT, so the lookup is exact; an
465		// endpoint not in this map is an unknown peer, which Send refuses rather than
466		// misrouting onto some other peer's paths. The primary is registered at construction;
467		// the concentrator registers each additional peer as it binds it. Read under m.mu on
468		// the Send path; written under m.mu (or at single-threaded construction).
469		peerByVirt map[*udpEndpoint]*peerState
470	
471		// shared is the SHARED per-socket path list (the sockets themselves), rebuilt from
472		// m.defs on every Open and mutated by the runtime add/remove. Each bound peer holds a
473		// peerPathState VIEW over a subset of these; on the single-peer edge/hub primary.paths
474		// is index-aligned with shared. len(shared)==0 is the "closed" (no sockets) state.
475		shared []*sharedPathState
476	
477		// deferred holds the configured paths whose well-formed source_addr was not yet
478		// assignable at the last Open (EADDRNOTAVAIL). They are NOT in shared or the
479		// scheduler — the tunnel runs on the paths that bound — but are recorded here,
480		// index-independent of shared, for the T55 background reconcile to retry as their
481		// addresses appear. Rebuilt from scratch on every Open; guarded by m.mu. The deferred-
482		// path machinery is SHARED (per-socket): a promoted deferred path fans its per-(peer,
483		// path) state out to every peer exactly as a runtime AddPath does.
484		deferred []deferredPath
485		// defaultRemote is the fallback per-path remote (the peer's wireguard
486		// endpoint) applied to any path without its own dest_addr. It may be set by
487		// ParseEndpoint BEFORE Open, so it is stored here and applied at Open time.
488		defaultRemote    netip.AddrPort
489		hasDefaultRemote bool
490	
491		// fecCfg / adaptiveCfg are the FEC configuration (T24/T29), shared by every peer; the
492		// per-peer fecSend/fecRecv runtime state Open builds from them lives on peerState. nil
493		// when FEC (respectively adaptive FEC) is disabled — the datapath is then byte-for-byte
494		// the pre-T24 behaviour. See fec.go.
495		fecCfg      *fec.Config
496		adaptiveCfg *adaptivefec.Config
497	
498		// Receive fan-in (T30). To let a path be added at runtime WITHOUT the engine
499		// spawning a new receive goroutine (it only builds its receive goroutines once,
500		// from the ReceiveFuncs Open returns), the per-path socket reads are decoupled
501		// from the engine-facing delivery: the Bind owns one readLoop goroutine PER path
502		// (tracked by readersWG) that reads its socket and feeds the shared resequencer,
503		// and Open returns a SINGLE engine-facing ReceiveFunc that drains the resequencer
504		// in order. A reader pokes deliverSignal after each datagram so the drainer wakes;
505		// recvClosed is closed by Close to release both the drainer and any reader parked
506		// on a delivery. openPort mirrors Open's bind port so a runtime-added path binds
507		// consistently; nextPathID is a monotonic high-water that persists ACROSS Open
508		// spans (a Close->Open never lowers it; only a process restart resets it), so a
509		// surviving path is never renumbered and a freed id is never reused for the
510		// process lifetime, and thus can never collide with the peer's per-path reflector
511		// state.
512		deliverSignal chan struct{}
513		recvClosed    chan struct{}
514		readersWG     sync.WaitGroup
515		openPort      uint16
516		nextPathID    uint16
517	
518		// Receive-path liveness sweep (T39, defect D15). Liveness DOWN-detection normally
519		// rides StartProbeLoop's single wall-clock ticker goroutine (emitProbes → Tick).
520		// Under heavy CPU load — e.g. the concentrator absorbing a saturating forward flood
521		// on 4 vCPU — that timer goroutine can be scheduled with ~1s jitter, delaying a
522		// path-DOWN transition (and thus the reply-direction failover) past the P1 budget.
523		// The per-path receive goroutines, in contrast, are the goroutines the inbound
524		// traffic is ALREADY scheduling, so driving Tick from them re-evaluates liveness
525		// even when the timer goroutine is starved. sweepIntervalNanos is the probe
526		// interval (0 until StartProbeLoop arms it — a bind without the probe transport
527		// never sweeps); lastSweepNanos is the wall-clock high-water throttling the sweep
528		// to at most once per interval so the receive hot path stays cheap.
529		sweepIntervalNanos atomic.Int64
530		lastSweepNanos     atomic.Int64
531	
532		// everUp is the STICKY "ever had a live path" predicate (I4): set true the first time
533		// ANY path, for ANY bound peer, reaches liveness telemetry.StateUp (dispatchInbound,
534		// on a fresh probe echo), and never cleared afterward — a later total outage is a
535		// genuine failure signal, not a startup warmup. The device-package engineLogger
536		// adapter consults EverHadLivePath to downgrade the startup no-healthy-path spam
537		// (ErrNoHealthyPath) to a single coalesced INFO line until the first path comes up,
538		// then lets it log at ERROR (a real outage) from then on.
539		everUp atomic.Bool
540	
541		// onFirstPathUp is the optional injectable one-shot callback (D37 detection seam):
542		// invoked EXACTLY ONCE, off the receive hot path, on the everUp latch's false->true
543		// edge — the SAME moment EverHadLivePath starts reporting true. It keeps the bind
544		// WG-unaware: dispatchInbound invokes an opaque func() rather than importing
545		// anything from the device/engine layer, so the device-layer consumer (the
546		// dependent task) wires whatever it needs (e.g. nudging the engine) through this
547		// closure. nil (the default, set by NewMultipath) means "no callback" — dispatchInbound
548		// checks for nil before invoking it, so an unset callback never panics. Set via
549		// SetOnFirstPathUp; read lock-free (atomic.Pointer) so the receive hot path never
550		// blocks on m.mu to check it.
551		onFirstPathUp atomic.Pointer[func()]
552	}
553	
554	// compile-time proof that Multipath satisfies the engine's Bind contract.
555	var _ Bind = (*Multipath)(nil)
556	
557	// NewMultipath returns a closed multipath Bind over the configured paths; call
558	// Open to bind the per-path sockets. The PSK keys the outer framing and must be
559	// set (config validation guarantees it). The scheduler is the injected send-side
560	// path-selection policy (T15) whose priority order MUST match the paths slice
561	// order (index 0 = the preferred primary); it is a required collaborator so the
562	// send path is never without a policy.
563	//
564	// probers is the per-path probe initiator that drives on-wire liveness (T13/T37).
565	// When non-nil it MUST hold exactly one *telemetry.Prober per path, in path order,
566	// and those SAME values MUST be the scheduler's PathHealth sources so the liveness
567	// the probe loop measures is the liveness the scheduler selects on. Pass nil to
568	// run the bind without the probe transport (the T12 unit tests drive selection via
569	// sched.AlwaysUp instead). A Reflector is built from the PSK to answer peer probes.
570	//
571	// newProber is the factory the runtime path-add path (AddPath, T30) uses to mint a
572	// prober for a newly-admitted path; it must be non-nil to allow AddPath and MUST be
573	// paired with a non-nil probers (the boot-time set) — a factory without a boot-time
574	// slice would let AddPath append to a nil m.probers, breaking the m.paths/m.probers
575	// alignment invariant and panicking on the next Open at m.probers[i]. Pass nil to
576	// forbid runtime path addition.
577	//
578	// fecCfg is the fixed-ratio Reed-Solomon FEC configuration (T24). Pass nil to run the
579	// datapath with FEC disabled (the pre-T24 behaviour, byte-for-byte); a non-nil,
580	// pre-validated config turns the send-side parity encoder and receive-side recovery
581	// decoder on. It is validated here (fail fast) so an invalid ratio is rejected at
582	// construction rather than at the first Open.
583	// adaptiveCfg is the adaptive-FEC controller configuration (T29). Pass nil for the
584	// fixed-ratio behaviour (T24); a non-nil config REQUIRES a non-nil fecCfg (the
585	// controller resizes the FEC encoder's parity — it is meaningless with the plane off)
586	// and reinterprets fecCfg.ParityShards as the controller's parity ceiling. It is
587	// validated here (fail fast) so a mis-tuned control law is rejected at construction.
588	//
589	// amnezia is the tunnel's AmneziaWG obfuscation profile (config.Amnezia). It
590	// parameterizes the send-path frame-type classifier so a WireGuard control frame is
591	// recognised and pacing-exempted under advanced security — custom magic headers and
592	// handshake junk prefixes — as well as in vanilla mode (defect D22). Pass the zero value
593	// for a vanilla (unobfuscated) tunnel; the classifier then uses the default type words
594	// and no junk prefix. It does NOT need to match the config's validated state — an
595	// all-zero profile is exactly the vanilla classifier.
596	func NewMultipath(paths []config.Path, psk config.Key, scheduler sched.Scheduler, probers []*telemetry.Prober, newProber ProberFactory, fecCfg *fec.Config, adaptiveCfg *adaptivefec.Config, amnezia config.Amnezia) (*Multipath, error) {
597		if len(paths) == 0 {
598			return nil, errors.New("bind: at least one path is required")
599		}
600		if !psk.IsSet() {
601			return nil, errors.New("bind: PSK is required for outer framing")
602		}
603		if len(paths) > 256 {
604			// path-id is a uint8 in the DATA frame header.
605			return nil, fmt.Errorf("bind: at most 256 paths supported, got %d", len(paths))
606		}
607		if scheduler == nil {
608			return nil, errors.New("bind: a send scheduler is required")
609		}
610		if newProber != nil && probers == nil {
611			// A runtime-path factory without a boot-time prober slice would let AddPath append
612			// to a nil m.probers, desyncing m.paths from m.probers and panicking on the next
613			// Open at m.probers[i]. The two are paired by construction; enforce it here.
614			return nil, errors.New("bind: newProber requires a non-nil probers (boot-time set)")
615		}
616		if probers != nil && len(probers) != len(paths) {
617			return nil, fmt.Errorf("bind: probers must have one entry per path (got %d, want %d)", len(probers), len(paths))
618		}
619		for i, pr := range probers {
620			if pr == nil {
621				return nil, fmt.Errorf("bind: prober %d is nil", i)
622			}
623		}
624		if fecCfg != nil {
625			if err := fecCfg.Validate(); err != nil {
626				return nil, fmt.Errorf("bind: invalid FEC configuration: %w", err)
627			}
628			if fecCfg.Deadline > maxFECDeadline {
629				// A deadline this large makes every deadline-flushed group's recovery
630				// structurally late: the resequencer skips the gap (resequencerTimeout) before
631				// the parity-derived frames land, so recovery never delivers. Reject it rather
632				// than ship a bond whose FEC silently cannot help (defect #4).
633				return nil, fmt.Errorf("bind: fec deadline %s exceeds the max %s (must stay safely below the resequencer's %s per-gap timeout so deadline-flushed recovery lands before the gap is skipped)", fecCfg.Deadline, maxFECDeadline, resequencerTimeout)
634			}
635		}
636		if adaptiveCfg != nil {
637			if fecCfg == nil {
638				return nil, errors.New("bind: adaptive FEC requires a FEC configuration (the controller resizes the FEC encoder's parity)")
639			}
640			if err := adaptiveCfg.Validate(); err != nil {
641				return nil, fmt.Errorf("bind: invalid adaptive FEC configuration: %w", err)
642			}
643			// The controller's parity ceiling (MaxParity) is the fixed FEC ParityShards both
644			// ends agree on: the receiver's decoder is built at fecCfg.ParityShards, and the
645			// encoder must never emit more parity than that ceiling (SetParity clamps to it).
646			// A mismatch would let the controller target a parity index the decoder rejects,
647			// so bind the two together at construction rather than trusting the caller.
648			if adaptiveCfg.MaxParity != fecCfg.ParityShards {
649				return nil, fmt.Errorf("bind: adaptive FEC parity ceiling (MaxParity=%d) must equal the FEC parity_shards (%d), which the receiver's decoder is built at", adaptiveCfg.MaxParity, fecCfg.ParityShards)
650			}
651			if adaptiveCfg.DataShards != fecCfg.DataShards {
652				return nil, fmt.Errorf("bind: adaptive FEC DataShards (%d) must equal the FEC data_shards (%d)", adaptiveCfg.DataShards, fecCfg.DataShards)
653			}
654		}
655		// The single-peer edge/hub constructs EXACTLY ONE peerState (the primary). virt is
656		// created here — NOT per Open — because the concentrator pins its destination once for
657		// the process life and every existing test relies on the virtual-endpoint pointer being
658		// stable across Open/Close/add/remove.
659		primary := newPeerState("", psk, scheduler, newProber, probers)
660		m := &Multipath{
661			defs:              append([]config.Path(nil), paths...),
662			classify:          newWGClassifier(amnezia),
663			deferredListen:    defaultDeferredListen,
664			resolveDeviceBind: resolveForcedDeviceBind,
665			addPathListen:     listenPath,
666			peerState:         primary,
667			peers:             []*peerState{primary},
668			peersByName:       map[string]*peerState{primary.name: primary},
669			peerByEndpoint:    map[netip.AddrPort]*peerState{},
670			peerByVirt:        map[*udpEndpoint]*peerState{primary.virt: primary},
671			maxDemuxSources:   defaultMaxDemuxSources,
672			fecCfg:            fecCfg,
673			adaptiveCfg:       adaptiveCfg,
674		}
675		// Publish the initial (primary-only) peer view the receive drainer iterates. No
676		// concurrency yet — the bind is not open — so this runs without m.mu.
677		m.republishPeersLocked()
678		return m, nil
679	}
680	
681	// republishPeersLocked snapshots m.peers into the lock-free peersView the engine-facing
682	// receive drainer (newReceiveFunc) iterates. The caller MUST hold m.mu whenever the bind
683	// can be open (so the snapshot never races a concurrent m.peers append); the constructor
684	// is the sole exception (it runs before the Multipath is reachable by any goroutine). The
685	// snapshot is a fresh slice so a later append to m.peers never mutates a view the drainer
686	// is mid-iteration over.
687	func (m *Multipath) republishPeersLocked() {
688		snap := make([]*peerState, len(m.peers))
689		copy(snap, m.peers)
690		m.peersView.Store(&snap)
691	}
692	
693	// AddConcentratorPeer registers one ADDITIONAL bound peer with the Bind — the concentrator's
694	// per-peer wiring (G4/T93). Peer 0 (the embedded primary) is built by NewMultipath; the
695	// concentrator calls this once per additional configured peer, each with its OWN effective psk,
696	// send scheduler, boot-time per-path prober set, and runtime prober factory (all keyed on that
697	// peer's psk, so one peer's codec/reflector reject another's frames — T84/R72). The peer's
698	// STABLE virtual endpoint is minted here (newPeerState) and registered in peerByVirt so an
699	// outbound Send routes replies back to THIS peer; Open then builds this peer's per-(peer,path)
700	// view of every bound socket, reconciles its scheduler, and (via newReceiveFunc) reports its
701	// virt to the engine on the first inbound frame (invariant A1: one virtual endpoint per peer).
702	//
703	// It MUST be called BEFORE Open (while the bind is closed): the per-(peer,path) views are
704	// rebuilt by Open from the registered peer set on every Open span (including each Close→Open
705	// cycle the engine drives on Down/Up and route changes), so a peer registered after the sockets
706	// are bound would be view-less and its DATA/PROBE never routed. probers, when supplied, must be
707	// index-aligned with the configured path membership (m.defs), exactly as the primary's are.
708	func (m *Multipath) AddConcentratorPeer(name string, psk config.Key, scheduler sched.Scheduler, probers []*telemetry.Prober, newProber ProberFactory) error {
709		if name == "" {
710			return errors.New("bind: concentrator peer name is required")
711		}
712		if !psk.IsSet() {
713			return errors.New("bind: concentrator peer psk is required")
714		}
715		if scheduler == nil {
716			return errors.New("bind: concentrator peer requires a send scheduler")
717		}
718		if newProber != nil && probers == nil {
719			return errors.New("bind: newProber requires a non-nil probers (boot-time set)")
720		}
721		m.mu.Lock()
722		defer m.mu.Unlock()
723		if len(m.paths) != 0 {
724			return errors.New("bind: concentrator peers must be registered before Open")
725		}
726		if probers != nil && len(probers) != len(m.defs) {
727			return fmt.Errorf("bind: concentrator peer %q: probers must have one entry per configured path (got %d, want %d)", name, len(probers), len(m.defs))
728		}
729		for i, pr := range probers {
730			if pr == nil {
731				return fmt.Errorf("bind: concentrator peer %q: prober %d is nil", name, i)
732			}
733		}
734		if name == m.name {
735			return fmt.Errorf("bind: concentrator peer name %q collides with the primary peer", name)
736		}
737		if _, dup := m.peersByName[name]; dup {
738			return fmt.Errorf("bind: duplicate concentrator peer name %q", name)
739		}
740		p := newPeerState(name, psk, scheduler, newProber, probers)
741		m.peers = append(m.peers, p)
742		m.peersByName[name] = p
743		m.peerByVirt[p.virt] = p
744		// Publish the grown peer set so the engine-facing receive drainer (newReceiveFunc) will
745		// enumerate this peer's resequencer once Open builds it.
746		m.republishPeersLocked()
747		return nil
748	}
749	
750	// Open binds one UDP socket per configured path to that path's source address on
751	// port (0 = random per socket), sets a large SO_RCVBUF on each, spawns one
752	// Bind-owned reader per path, and returns a SINGLE engine-facing ReceiveFunc (the
753	// fan-in drainer) plus the first path's bound port. Each path whose config carries
754	// a dest_addr — or, failing that, the peer's wireguard endpoint learned via
755	// ParseEndpoint — starts with a known remote; the rest are learned from inbound
756	// traffic.
757	//
758	// Open is the sole creator of the per-path sockets: the engine's bring-up path
759	// calls Close() first (on the still-unopened bind) and then Open(), so building
760	// the sockets HERE — not in NewMultipath — is what makes that Close→Open sequence
761	// (and every subsequent Down→Up) work. The engine passes the previously-bound
762	// port back on a re-Open; each path binds to it on its own distinct source
763	// address, and the first path's bound port is returned so the engine keeps a
764	// stable listen port across the cycle (matching conn.StdNetBind).
765	func (m *Multipath) Open(port uint16) ([]ReceiveFunc, uint16, error) {
766		m.mu.Lock()
767		defer m.mu.Unlock()
768		if len(m.paths) != 0 {
769			return nil, 0, conn.ErrBindAlreadyOpen
770		}
771	
772		// (Re)build the per-Open datapath planes — send Codec, receive resequencer, and FEC
773		// send/receive state — fresh for this bring-up, for EVERY bound peer (not just the
774		// primary via promotion). This keeps Open symmetric with closeSocketsLocked, which
775		// clears every peer's per-Open state: a concentrator peer bound before Open must get
776		// its OWN fresh planes on each Close→Open cycle, and one peer's (re)creation must never
777		// touch another peer's release point or FEC group state. On the single-peer edge/hub
778		// m.peers holds only the primary, so this is byte-identical to the pre-split rebuild.
779		// See openPeerDatapathLocked.
780		for _, p := range m.peers {
781			if err := m.openPeerDatapathLocked(p); err != nil {
782				_ = m.closeSocketsLocked()
783				return nil, 0, err
784			}
785		}
786	
787		// Resolve every path's interface up front and decide, per path, whether its
788		// socket may be device-bound (SO_BINDTODEVICE + wildcard — survives a T16
789		// re-roam) or must pin the specific source IP (the pre-T16 behaviour, required
790		// when paths share an interface or the interface is multi-address, so distinct
791		// specific-IP sockets coexist on one port without an EADDRINUSE collision). See
792		// selectDeviceBinds.
793		srcs := make([]netip.Addr, len(m.defs))
794		modes := make([]config.BindMode, len(m.defs))
795		for i := range m.defs {
796			srcs[i] = m.defs[i].SourceAddr
797			modes[i] = m.defs[i].Bind
798		}
799		bindDevs := planPathBinds(srcs, modes)
800	
801		// A path whose WELL-FORMED source_addr is merely NOT-YET-ASSIGNABLE at boot
802		// (EADDRNOTAVAIL: no interface holds the address — a 5G modem without a DHCP
803		// lease, Starlink mid-obstruction) is DEFERRED rather than treated as fatal, so
804		// the bond comes up on the paths that DO bind (G2/W1, approach A). Tolerating it
805		// requires the runtime path-down machinery: the deferred path's prober stays
806		// StateDown so the scheduler excludes it, and a DynamicScheduler whose membership
807		// can be reconciled to only the bound paths. Absent the probe transport (the T12
808		// no-prober unit binds) or a dynamic scheduler there is no such Down model, so —
809		// exactly as AddPath refuses a runtime add without probers — every bind error
810		// stays fatal there. A MALFORMED source_addr never reaches here (config.validate
811		// rejects it at load), and any OTHER bind error (EADDRINUSE, permission) is fatal.
812		_, dynOK := m.scheduler.(sched.DynamicScheduler)
813		tolerateDefer := m.probers != nil && dynOK
814		m.deferred = nil
815	
816		actualPort := port
817		firstBound := true
818		for i := range m.defs {
819			def := m.defs[i]
820			// Device-bind this path when selectDeviceBinds proved it safe (so a
821			// mid-session source-address change / T16 re-roam does not break the socket),
822			// otherwise pin the specific source IP. See selectDeviceBinds / listenPath.
823			c, err := listenPath(def.SourceAddr, port, bindDevs[i])
824			if err != nil {
825				if tolerateDefer && errors.Is(err, syscall.EADDRNOTAVAIL) {
826					// Defer this path: record its def + boot prober (kept Down) for the T55
827					// background reconcile to retry, and leave the bond to come up on the rest.
828					// Mirrors AddPath's rollback discipline — a failed path never disturbs the
829					// tunnel — at the boot boundary.
830					m.deferred = append(m.deferred, deferredPath{def: def, prober: m.probers[i]})
831					continue
832				}
833				_ = m.closeSocketsLocked()
834				return nil, 0, fmt.Errorf("bind: open path %q on %s: %w", def.Name, def.SourceAddr, err)
835			}
836			// Large SO_RCVBUF, best-effort: the kernel caps at net.core.rmem_max and
837			// SetReadBuffer does not require privilege, so a returned error is rare;
838			// treat it as non-fatal rather than refusing to bind in a restricted env.
839			_ = c.SetReadBuffer(socketRecvBuffer)
840	
841			shared := &sharedPathState{name: def.Name, id: uint8(i), src: def.SourceAddr, conn: c}
842			// Build EVERY bound peer's view of this shared socket (T93): each peer decodes under its
843			// OWN psk-derived Codec and probes with its OWN per-(peer,path) prober, so a concentrator
844			// socket shared by several peers keeps each peer's authenticated stream isolated
845			// (demuxInbound source-demuxes on the first authenticated PROBE). The primary (peers[0])
846			// is built exactly as the pre-split single peer — byte-identical on the single-peer
847			// edge/hub, where m.peers holds only the primary. m.paths (the primary's path slice,
848			// reached through the embed) grows via the pi==0 append below; a concentrator peer's
849			// paths grow on its own peerState.
850			for pi, p := range m.peers {
851				// The path binds to peer p; its receive codec is p's codec (derived from p's psk).
852				codec, err := p.newCodec()
853				if err != nil {
854					_ = c.Close()
855					_ = m.closeSocketsLocked()
856					return nil, 0, err
857				}
858				pp := &peerPathState{sharedPathState: shared, peer: p, codec: codec}
859				if p.probers != nil {
860					// Fail fast rather than panic if a peer's prober slice ever falls short of the
861					// shared m.defs membership: every runtime admission (bound OR deferred) fans a
862					// per-peer prober out to EVERY peer (AddPath), so p.probers stays index-aligned
863					// with m.defs. A divergence is a wiring defect — surface it as a bind error
864					// instead of an index-out-of-range panic that would crash the daemon.
865					if i >= len(p.probers) {
866						_ = c.Close()
867						_ = m.closeSocketsLocked()
868						return nil, 0, fmt.Errorf("bind: peer %q prober set (len %d) is shorter than the path membership at index %d — per-peer prober fan-out desync", p.name, len(p.probers), i)
869					}
870					pp.prober = p.probers[i]
871					if pi == 0 {
872						// Reconcile the SHARED DATA-frame path-id to the PRIMARY prober's IMMUTABLE
873						// stamp rather than the slice index i: after a runtime RemovePath the survivor
874						// keeps its original (higher) stamp, so index-based numbering would renumber a
875						// live path AND diverge its DATA id from its PROBE stamp. Every peer's
876						// probers[i] carries the SAME stamp (the device stamps each peer's boot prober
877						// for path i identically), so taking it from the primary is authoritative and
878						// keeps DATA and every peer's PROBE agreeing on the wire.
879						shared.id = pp.prober.PathID()
880					}
881				}
882				switch {
883				case def.DestAddr.IsValid():
884					pp.setRemote(def.DestAddr)
885				case m.hasDefaultRemote:
886					pp.setRemote(m.defaultRemote)
887				}
888				p.paths = append(p.paths, pp)
889				// Publish this peer's view for the receive demux (T88). A single-view socket is the
890				// edge/hub byte-identical fast path in demuxInbound; a socket with >1 view is
891				// source-demuxed to its owning peer on each authenticated PROBE.
892				shared.addViewLocked(pp)
893			}
894			m.shared = append(m.shared, shared)
895			if firstBound {
896				actualPort = uint16(c.LocalAddr().(*net.UDPAddr).Port)
897				firstBound = false
898			}
899		}
900	
901		// Hard guard (a): a bond with NO transport is impossible. If every configured path
902		// deferred, tolerance must NOT degrade to a zero-path bind — fail fatally, exactly
903		// as the pre-tolerance Open did when the sole path could not bind.
904		if len(m.paths) == 0 {
905			_ = m.closeSocketsLocked()
906			return nil, 0, fmt.Errorf("bind: no configured path could bind its source address (all %d deferred as not-yet-assignable)", len(m.deferred))
907		}
908	
909		// nextPathID is a MONOTONIC HIGH-WATER carried ACROSS Open spans, never decreased.
910		// Close does not reset it, and here it is only ever RAISED to at least max(existing
911		// path stamp)+1. Resetting it to len(m.paths) (the pre-fix behaviour) re-minted an id
912		// a survivor still held once a RemovePath had opened a gap in the stamp space below
913		// that survivor's stamp: the next runtime AddPath then collided with the live path at
914		// an identical (PathID, SessionID), and because the peer's Reflector keys anti-replay
915		// AND the session challenge PER PathID, the strict-monotonic replay filter dropped one
916		// of the two independent ProbeSeq streams -> probe loss / false-DOWN. Deriving the
917		// high-water from the (prober-stamped) ps.id covers both the probe-transport case and
918		// the T12 no-prober case (where ps.id == i, so the high-water is len(m.paths)).
919		m.openPort = port
920		for _, ps := range m.paths {
921			if uint16(ps.id)+1 > m.nextPathID {
922				m.nextPathID = uint16(ps.id) + 1
923			}
924		}
925		// A DEFERRED path's boot prober already OWNS its path-id stamp; raise the
926		// high-water past it too, so a runtime AddPath arriving while the path is still
927		// deferred cannot mint that reserved id and collide with it once T55 binds it.
928		for _, dp := range m.deferred {
929			if dp.prober != nil {
930				if uint16(dp.prober.PathID())+1 > m.nextPathID {
931					m.nextPathID = uint16(dp.prober.PathID()) + 1
932				}
933			}
934		}
935	
936		// Reconcile the scheduler's membership with the path slice just rebuilt from
937		// m.defs. A runtime AddPath/RemovePath (T30) keeps m.defs, m.probers, AND the
938		// live scheduler in lockstep during an Open span; re-pinning the scheduler's
939		// health list to the BOUND probers HERE — index-aligned with m.paths, since each
940		// bound path appended its ps.prober to boundProbers in order above — is the single
941		// reconciliation point that makes that runtime membership survive this
942		// Close→Open cycle without a scheduler/path desync (no frozen zombie health
943		// entry, no resurrected removed path). A DEFERRED path (not-yet-assignable
944		// source_addr) is deliberately EXCLUDED here: its prober is absent from the
945		// scheduler, so Pick can never select it, and Send's Pick->m.paths[idx] mapping
946		// stays index-aligned with the bound-only path slice. Only meaningful with the
947		// probe transport: a bind without probers cannot change membership at runtime
948		// (AddPath is refused) and never defers, so its scheduler is left exactly as built.
949		// Reconcile EACH bound peer's scheduler membership with the path slice just rebuilt from
950		// m.defs (T93): every peer's per-(peer,path) views were appended in m.defs order above, so a
951		// peer's scheduler health list is that peer's BOUND probers in order (a DEFERRED path
952		// contributed no view and is deliberately excluded, exactly as for the primary). On the
953		// single-peer edge/hub m.peers holds only the primary, so this reconciles exactly the one
954		// scheduler — byte-identical to the pre-split single reconcile. A peer without the probe
955		// transport or with a non-dynamic scheduler is left as built (the T12 no-prober unit binds
956		// never change membership at runtime and never defer).
957		for _, p := range m.peers {
958			pdyn, pdynOK := p.scheduler.(sched.DynamicScheduler)
959			if p.probers == nil || !pdynOK {
960				continue
961			}
962			health := make([]sched.PathHealth, 0, len(p.paths))
963			for _, pp := range p.paths {
964				if pp.prober != nil {
965					health = append(health, pp.prober)
966				}
967			}
968			if err := pdyn.SetPaths(health); err != nil {
969				_ = m.closeSocketsLocked()
970				return nil, 0, fmt.Errorf("bind: reconcile scheduler on open: %w", err)
971			}
972		}
973	
974		// Stand up the receive fan-in: one Bind-owned reader per path feeding the shared
975		// resequencer, and a single engine-facing drainer. Both channels are recreated
976		// per Open so a Close→Open cycle starts clean.
977		m.deliverSignal = make(chan struct{}, 1)
978		m.recvClosed = make(chan struct{})
979		for _, ps := range m.paths {
980			m.readersWG.Add(1)
981			go m.readLoop(ps, m.deliverSignal)
982		}
983		// Deadline-tick goroutine for FEC group close (T24): it flushes a partial group's
984		// parity on time under low load so a group is never stranded waiting for the size
985		// threshold. Tracked by readersWG (like the readers) so Close waits for it; it
986		// exits on recvClosed. It TryLocks m.mu, so it never blocks Close's readersWG.Wait
987		// held under m.mu.
988		if m.fecSend.Load() != nil {
989			m.readersWG.Add(1)
990			go m.fecTickLoop(m.fecCfg.Deadline, m.recvClosed)
991		}
992		return []ReceiveFunc{m.newReceiveFunc(m.deliverSignal, m.recvClosed)}, actualPort, nil
993	}
994	
995	// openPeerDatapathLocked (re)builds ONE peer's per-Open datapath planes: its send Codec
996	// (derived from THIS peer's psk), its receive resequencer, and — when FEC is configured —
997	// its FEC send/receive planes. Each is fresh per Open so a Close→Open cycle (or reconnect)
998	// re-pins the peer's release point and re-anchors its FEC grouping to this bring-up rather
999	// than a stale prior span. It writes ONLY the given peer's fields (from the peer's own psk
1000	// and the bind-wide fecCfg/adaptiveCfg), so one peer's (re)creation never touches another
1001	// peer's resequencer or FEC group state — the per-peer lifecycle boundary that keeps a
1002	// reconnect on one peer from disturbing another's. Caller holds m.mu; on error the caller
1003	// unwinds the whole Open via closeSocketsLocked (which clears every peer's per-Open state),
1004	// so a partial build here is cleaned up.
1005	func (m *Multipath) openPeerDatapathLocked(ps *peerState) error {
1006		sendCodec, err := ps.newCodec()
1007		if err != nil {
1008			return err
1009		}
1010	
1011		// A fresh resequencer per Open: its release point re-pins to the first frame THIS peer
1012		// receives after this bring-up, so a Close→Open cycle (or reconnect) never wedges on a
1013		// stale high-water outer-seq. Published atomically so the peer's per-path readLoop
1014		// goroutines read it WITHOUT m.mu.

4	# and delete what you do not need:

9	# 0600 is MANDATORY: the file holds the WireGuard private key and the PSK, so
10	# config.Load refuses any file whose mode is not exactly 0600.
11	#
12	# This example is EDGE-oriented. For a CONCENTRATOR: set role = "concentrator",
13	# uncomment [wireguard].listen_port, and REMOVE the peer `endpoint`/`endpoints`
14	# (the concentrator learns the edge's endpoints from authenticated traffic).
15	#

17	# DEFAULT value (uncommenting one at its shown value is a no-op). Cross-field
18	# constraints a single example cannot express inline are listed at the bottom.
19	# Full prose reference: docs/install.md §3z. Everything here is enforced at
20	# config load (fail-fast on the first violation) except the loopback + allowed_ips
21	# checks noted below.

25	                                   #   inferred from other keys.

28	                                   #   authenticates outer PROBE/CONTROL frames.

310	# timeout = "5s"
311	#
312	# EXAMPLE 3 (system resolver, any IP-literal endpoint):
313	# [dns]
314	# resolver = "system"              # DEFAULT "system". No URL/server/bootstrap needed.
315	# poll_interval = "30s"
316	# timeout = "5s"
317	#
318	# Each EXAMPLE above is self-contained and independently valid: uncomment
319	# EXACTLY ONE (its [dns] header plus its fields) and nothing else. The three
320	# resolver modes are mutually exclusive — setting fields from more than one
321	# EXAMPLE at once (e.g. resolver = "doh" together with dot_server, or a
322	# bootstrap_ip on a doh_url/dot_server host that is already an IP literal) is
323	# rejected at config load. See internal/config/dns.go for every field's exact
324	# precondition.
325	
326	# ── metrics: OPTIONAL. Omit the block => no /metrics endpoint is served ───────
327	[metrics]
328	listen = "127.0.0.1:9090"          # No default. LOOPBACK-ONLY (127.0.0.0/8, ::1, or
329	                                   #   a hostname resolving only to loopback); any
330	                                   #   other address is REFUSED when the endpoint
331	                                   #   binds (not at load).
332	
333	# ── log: OPTIONAL ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
334	[log]
335	level = "info"                     # DEFAULT "info" (empty => info). One of
336	                                   #   debug | info | warn | error ("warning"
337	                                   #   accepted for warn). Unknown => fail-fast.
338	
339	# ── Cross-field constraints (not expressible inline) ─────────────────────────
340	# • Roles: `role` is required, never inferred. Edge-only: per-peer endpoint/
341	#   endpoints (required on edge, rejected on concentrator) + the pacing
342	#   declarations (dest_addr is edge-only in effect, merely inert on the
343	#   concentrator). Concentrator-only: wireguard.listen_port (required there).
344	# • endpoint vs endpoints: mutually exclusive; `endpoint` is the one-element form
345	#   of `endpoints`. On the edge exactly one must be present; the concentrator sets
346	#   neither. Each entry is an ip:port literal (default) or, with the peer's
347	#   `dns = true` opt-in, a hostname:port resolved at runtime (never at config
348	#   load). Duplicates are rejected within each form (literal-vs-literal,
349	#   hostname:port-vs-hostname:port) — a hostname is never compared against a
350	#   literal at load time.
351	# • Distinct source_addr: each [[paths]] needs a unique, valid source_addr
352	#   (compared unmapped, so 192.0.2.10 and ::ffff:192.0.2.10 collide). `name` must
353	#   also be unique.
354	# • allowed_ips: >= 1 CIDR per peer; empty is rejected when the WG config is
355	#   assembled (not by config.validate).
356	# • amnezia all-or-nothing: an unconfigured (all-zero) block is plain WireGuard.
357	#   Once ANY of jc/jmin/jmax/s1/s2/h1..h4 is set, the full jc,jmin,jmax,s1,s2 set
358	#   must be > 0, jmin <= jmax, 148+s1 != 92+s2, and h1..h4 must be a distinct set
359	#   (omitted headers default to 1..4). Same profile on both ends. One engine/process.
360	# • link_bandwidth + link_rtt are a pair: both-or-neither, and all-or-nothing
361	#   across every path (declare on every [[paths]] block or none). They take effect
362	#   ONLY under scheduler.policy = "weighted" with pacing_enabled = true; otherwise
363	#   a declared bandwidth is inert. When active they are mutually exclusive with the
364	#   raw per_path_capacity_fps / pacing_burst_frames knobs.
365	# • scheduler off-unless-present: no [scheduler] => active-backup, every weighted
366	#   knob ignored. Under weighted, the hysteresis band requires
367	#   disengage_fraction < engage_fraction.
368	# • fec off-unless-present: no [fec] (or enabled = false) => no parity. adaptive =
369	#   true requires enabled = true. In adaptive mode set EITHER target_residual OR
370	#   safety_factor, never both; both must stay 0 (unset) in fixed mode.
371	# • metrics loopback-only: listen must be a loopback address (or a hostname
372	#   resolving entirely to loopback); a non-loopback bind is refused when the
373	#   endpoint starts. Omit the block to serve no metrics.
374	# • bind (top-level default + per-path override): "source" | "device" | "auto"
375	#   (DEFAULT). A [[paths]] block with no `bind` uses the top-level default
376	#   (itself DEFAULT "auto" when also omitted, so an existing config with no
377	#   `bind` anywhere is unaffected). "auto" reproduces today's selectDeviceBinds
378	#   heuristic; "source" and "device" force one behavior unconditionally. See
379	#   install.md §3b for the VLAN-per-WAN policy-routing case `bind = "source"`
380	#   exists to fix.
381	# • dns off-unless-present: no [dns] => system resolver, default cadence/
382	#   timeouts; [dns] only selects the transport a peer's `dns = true` opt-in
383	#   uses, never a gate by itself. doh_url/dot_server are mode-specific: set
384	#   exactly the one matching `resolver`, the other (and bootstrap_ip under
385	#   resolver = "system") must stay absent. BOOTSTRAP-IP invariant: a
386	#   hostname-form doh_url/dot_server host REQUIRES bootstrap_ip; an IP
387	#   literal host REJECTS a non-empty bootstrap_ip (mode mismatch).
388

# wanbond.example.toml — exhaustive annotated configuration reference.
#
# Every key wanbond reads is present here. Copy this file, fill the <placeholders>,
# and delete what you do not need:
#
#   sudo install -m 0600 /dev/null /etc/wanbond/edge.toml
#   $EDITOR /etc/wanbond/edge.toml     # paste + edit this content
#
# 0600 is MANDATORY: the file holds the WireGuard private key and the PSK, so
# config.Load refuses any file whose mode is not exactly 0600.
#
# This example is EDGE-oriented. For a CONCENTRATOR: set role = "concentrator",
# uncomment [wireguard].listen_port, and REMOVE the peer `endpoint`/`endpoints`
# (the concentrator learns the edge's endpoints from authenticated traffic).
#
# Required keys are shown live; optional keys are commented-out with their
# DEFAULT value (uncommenting one at its shown value is a no-op). Cross-field
# constraints a single example cannot express inline are listed at the bottom.
# Full prose reference: docs/install.md §3z. Everything here is enforced at
# config load (fail-fast on the first violation) except the loopback + allowed_ips
# checks noted below.

# ── top level ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
role = "edge"                      # REQUIRED. "edge" | "concentrator". Never
                                   #   inferred from other keys.
psk  = "<base64 32-byte PSK>"      # REQUIRED. 32 raw bytes, base64. SAME value on
                                   #   both ends; keys the PSK-HMAC that
                                   #   authenticates outer PROBE/CONTROL frames.
                                   #   Generate: head -c 32 /dev/urandom | base64
# tun_persist = false              # OPTIONAL, DEFAULT false. When false the TUN
                                   #   wanbond0 is destroyed on daemon stop, so its
                                   #   addresses/routes/rules are dropped on every
                                   #   restart (today's teardown semantics). Set
                                   #   true and the daemon issues TUNSETPERSIST on
                                   #   start: wanbond0 SURVIVES a daemon restart
                                   #   with the SAME ifindex, so operator-owned
                                   #   addressing persists untouched. NOTE (D39): a
                                   #   persistent device STILL needs the
                                   #   NetworkManager unmanaged-devices drop-in on
                                   #   NM hosts — persistence keeps the link, it
                                   #   does not exempt it from NM.
# bind = "auto"                    # OPTIONAL, DEFAULT "auto". Top-level default
                                   #   bind mode, applied to every [[paths]] block
                                   #   that omits its own `bind` below. "source" |
                                   #   "device" | "auto" — see the per-path `bind`
                                   #   note below for what each value does. Exists
                                   #   for the VLAN-per-WAN policy-routing case;
                                   #   see docs/install.md §3b.

# ── paths: one [[paths]] block per WAN uplink; at least one is REQUIRED ───────
[[paths]]
name        = "starlink"           # REQUIRED. Stable, unique identifier.
source_addr = "192.168.1.10"       # REQUIRED. Bare local source IP the path's UDP
                                   #   socket binds to. Must be unique across paths
                                   #   (a shared source collides EADDRINUSE at the
                                   #   second bind). No default.
# bind = "auto"                    # OPTIONAL, DEFAULT the top-level default above
                                   #   (itself DEFAULT "auto" when that is also
                                   #   omitted). "source" forces the pre-T16
                                   #   source-IP pin unconditionally (defeats
                                   #   device-bind roam tolerance, but is the fix
                                   #   for a one-address-per-VLAN policy-routing
                                   #   edge — see docs/install.md §3b). "device"
                                   #   forces SO_BINDTODEVICE unconditionally.
                                   #   "auto" reproduces today's heuristic:
                                   #   device-bind only when provably equivalent
                                   #   to the source-IP pin, source-bind otherwise.
# dest_addr = "203.0.113.7:51820"  # OPTIONAL, edge-only meaning. Per-path
                                   #   concentrator endpoint (ip:port). Omit when
                                   #   one public IP fronts all uplinks (the peer's
                                   #   endpoint is reused). No default. Inert on the
                                   #   concentrator (it learns edge endpoints).
# link_bandwidth = "50Mbit"        # OPTIONAL. Operator-declared bottleneck
                                   #   bandwidth. SI bit/s: k/M/G = 1e3/1e6/1e9
                                   #   ("bit" may be written "bps"). Must be > 0.
                                   #   Used ONLY under weighted policy + pacing;
                                   #   inert otherwise. No default (undeclared).
# link_rtt = "45ms"                # OPTIONAL. Operator-declared baseline RTT (Go
                                   #   duration). REQUIRED (> 0) when link_bandwidth
                                   #   is set under weighted pacing; else ignored.

# A second uplink (edge). Repeat the block per path.
[[paths]]
name        = "5g"
source_addr = "192.168.2.10"

# ── wireguard: inner tunnel key material ─────────────────────────────────────
[wireguard]
private_key = "<base64 32-byte private key>"  # REQUIRED (both roles).
                                   #   Generate: wg genkey (public: … | wg pubkey)
# listen_port = 51820              # CONCENTRATOR-ONLY: REQUIRED (> 0) there; omit /
                                   #   leave 0 on the edge. uint16 (< 1024 needs
                                   #   CAP_NET_BIND_SERVICE).

# ── wireguard peers: at least one [[wireguard.peers]] is REQUIRED ─────────────
[[wireguard.peers]]
public_key = "<base64 peer public key>"  # REQUIRED. base64 32-byte key (the OTHER
                                   #   end's public key).
endpoint = "203.0.113.7:51820"     # EDGE: REQUIRED (this OR `endpoints`). ip:port
                                   #   literal (default) OR hostname with opt-in
                                   #   `dns = true` below. CONCENTRATOR: must be
                                   #   ABSENT (rejected) — it roams the edge
                                   #   dynamically. Mutually exclusive with
                                   #   `endpoints`.
# dns = false                       # OPTIONAL, DEFAULT false. Set true to enable
                                   #   hostname resolution for the endpoint above.
                                   #   The [dns] block below is OPTIONAL: it only
                                   #   selects the resolver TRANSPORT and defaults
                                   #   to the system resolver when absent.
# endpoints = ["203.0.113.7:51820", "198.51.100.7:51820"]
                                   # EDGE-ONLY ordered hub-failover list: [0] is the
                                   #   active concentrator, the rest ordered
                                   #   standbys. Each may be an ip:port literal
                                   #   (default) or a hostname with dns = true; when
                                   #   hostnames are used, each resolves to its full
                                   #   A+AAAA set and all addresses are ordered
                                   #   together in the failover list. duplicates
                                   #   rejected. Mutually exclusive with `endpoint`
                                   #   (its one-element form). Rejected on the
                                   #   concentrator.
allowed_ips = ["10.77.0.1/32"]     # REQUIRED: >= 1 CIDR routed to this peer
                                   #   (enforced when the WG UAPI is built). This is
                                   #   the peer's inner tunnel address(es). A literal
                                   #   0.0.0.0/0 or ::/0 (full tunnel) is ALWAYS split
                                   #   into the equivalent /1+/1 pair at UAPI render —
                                   #   the engine never sees the literal /0.
# mode = "default-route"           # OPTIONAL, edge-only. Marks this peer as the
                                   #   edge's full-tunnel concentrator (opts into a
                                   #   0.0.0.0/0 / ::/0 allowed_ips entry above).
                                   #   Rejected on the concentrator. When set, the
                                   #   daemon installs this peer's allowed_ips split
                                   #   (wg-quick /1+/1 for 0.0.0.0/0) as scope-link
                                   #   routes via wanbond0 once the interface is up,
                                   #   and removes them on stop. Routes ONLY — no
                                   #   policy routing, SNAT, or concentrator
                                   #   forwarding (those stay operator-owned).
# psk = "<base64 32-byte PSK>"      # OPTIONAL. Meaningful only for a MULTI-PEER
                                   #   concentrator (see the example below). With
                                   #   exactly one peer configured — as shown live
                                   #   here — a per-peer psk is REJECTED at load
                                   #   ("psk is not meaningful with a single peer");
                                   #   leave it UNSET and the top-level `psk` above
                                   #   is used, so a legacy single-peer config is
                                   #   unaffected. With MORE THAN ONE peer, this
                                   #   field becomes REQUIRED and must be pairwise-
                                   #   distinct across peers: each peer authenticates
                                   #   its own PROBE frames with its OWN psk, and the
                                   #   top-level `psk` authenticates NO peer once a
                                   #   second peer is configured (T80/T81, Q21).
                                   #   Generate: head -c 32 /dev/urandom | base64
# name = "edge1"                    # OPTIONAL. Unused when a single peer is
                                   #   configured, as shown live here (metrics never
                                   #   carry a `peer` label for a single bound peer,
                                   #   see docs/runbook.md's "Multi-peer concentrator
                                   #   (G4)" metrics note). With MORE THAN ONE peer,
                                   #   REQUIRED and must be unique across peers
                                   #   (T80/T81, Q21). ADDITIONAL peers (all but the
                                   #   first-configured) are exposed under this name
                                   #   as the metrics `peer` label; the first/primary
                                   #   peer's series always carry the empty label
                                   #   peer="" regardless of this field (D58).

# ── second peer example (hostname endpoint with DNS opt-in) ──────────────────────
# [[wireguard.peers]]
# public_key = "<base64 peer public key>"
# endpoint = "concentrator.example.com:51820"  # hostname endpoint (requires dns = true)
# dns = true                        # enable hostname resolution for this endpoint
#                                   #   ([dns] block below is OPTIONAL — absent
#                                   #   means the system resolver; see [dns])
# allowed_ips = ["10.77.0.1/32"]

# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# MULTI-PEER CONCENTRATOR EXAMPLE (G4): TWO EDGES, ONE CONCENTRATOR
# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
#
# The plural [[wireguard.peers]] syntax (one block per edge) is supported on the
# concentrator. Single-peer configs (one [[wireguard.peers]] block, as shown
# live above) are unaffected: with exactly one peer, a per-peer `psk` is
# REJECTED at load (the top-level `psk` is used) and a per-peer `name` is
# optional and inert for metrics.
#
# With MORE THAN ONE peer, every peer authenticates its own PROBE frames with
# its OWN per-peer `psk` (internal/bind/multipath.go's peerBySource demux binds
# a source only once a PROBE MAC-verifies under that peer's psk); the
# top-level `psk` below remains REQUIRED by config validation but
# authenticates NO peer once a second peer is configured
# (internal/device/device.go feeds only per-peer PSKs to the bind — see
# docs/design.md's Security model). Each peer's `name` is REQUIRED and must be
# unique; ADDITIONAL peers (all but the first-configured) are exposed under
# their configured name as the metrics `peer` label, while the first/primary
# peer's series still carry the empty label peer="" — byte-compatible with the
# single-peer exposition, but a discrepancy versus the "name required" config
# rule filed as defect D58 (documented current behavior; not fixed here).
#
# ── live example (two edges, distinct per-peer psk/name) ─────────────────────
# role = "concentrator"
# psk = "<base64 32-byte PSK>"      # REQUIRED by validate; authenticates no
                                   #   peer once a second peer is configured.
#
# [[paths]]
# name = "wan0"
# source_addr = "10.0.0.5"
#
# [wireguard]
# private_key = "<base64 32-byte private key>"
# listen_port = 51820
#
# # Edge 1: office — bound FIRST, so its metrics series carry peer="" (D58)
# # even though `name` is set and required in multi-peer mode.
# [[wireguard.peers]]
# public_key = "<base64 office-edge public key>"
# allowed_ips = ["10.77.1.0/24"]
# psk = "<base64 32-byte PSK for office-edge>"    # REQUIRED in multi-peer mode
# name = "office-edge"                             # REQUIRED in multi-peer mode
#
# # Edge 2: mobile — registered via AddConcentratorPeer, so its metrics
# # series carry peer="mobile-edge".
# [[wireguard.peers]]
# public_key = "<base64 mobile-edge public key>"
# allowed_ips = ["10.77.2.0/24"]
# psk = "<base64 32-byte PSK for mobile-edge>"    # Must differ from office-edge's psk
# name = "mobile-edge"                             # Must differ from office-edge's name
#
# [metrics]
# listen = "127.0.0.1:9090"

# ── amnezia obfuscation: OPTIONAL, OFF by default (plain WireGuard) ───────────
# ALL-OR-NOTHING: either omit the whole block, or set the entire jc/jmin/jmax/s1/s2
# set — IDENTICALLY on both ends. One obfuscation engine per process.
# [amnezia]
# jc   = 4                         # junk packet count. > 0 when configured.
# jmin = 40                        # min junk packet size. > 0; jmin <= jmax.
# jmax = 70                        # max junk packet size. > 0.
# s1   = 30                        # init-packet junk prefix size. > 0.
# s2   = 40                        # response-packet junk prefix size. > 0.
                                   #   Constraint: (148 + s1) != (92 + s2) — the
                                   #   obfuscated init/response lengths must differ.
                                   #   (no defaults: required when the block present.)
# h1 = 1                           # magic header: initiation. Default 1..4 when the
# h2 = 2                           #   block is configured but NO header is given.
# h3 = 3                           #   When any is set, h1..h4 must be a DISTINCT set
# h4 = 4                           #   (values <= 4 mean "standard message type").

# ── scheduler: OPTIONAL. Omitted => active-backup, all knobs below ignored ────
# [scheduler]
# policy = "active-backup"         # "active-backup" (DEFAULT) | "weighted". Every
                                   #   knob below applies ONLY to "weighted"; under
                                   #   active-backup they are inert and left unset.
# per_path_capacity_fps = 10000.0  # DEFAULT 10000. Reference per-path capacity
                                   #   (frame slots/s): aggregation-gate denominator
                                   #   and pacing refill rate. > 0.
# engage_fraction    = 0.9         # DEFAULT 0.9. Engage aggregation above
                                   #   engage_fraction * capacity. In (0, 1].
# disengage_fraction = 0.5         # DEFAULT 0.5. Collapse below disengage_fraction
                                   #   * capacity. Must be in [0, engage_fraction) —
                                   #   the hysteresis band.
# collapse_dwell = "2s"            # DEFAULT 2s. Sustained-low dwell before
                                   #   collapsing to primary-only. >= 0.
# load_tau = "200ms"               # DEFAULT 200ms. Offered-load rate estimator time
                                   #   constant. > 0.
# pacing_enabled = false           # DEFAULT false. Turn on per-path send-pacing
                                   #   (token buckets). Off => buckets bypassed.
# pacing_burst_frames = 64.0       # DEFAULT 64. Token-bucket burst (frames). > 0
                                   #   when pacing_enabled.
# weight_rtt_floor = "1ms"         # DEFAULT 1ms. RTT floor in the weight formula. >0.
# weight_loss_floor = 0.001        # DEFAULT 1e-3. Loss floor under the sqrt in the
                                   #   weight formula. > 0.

# ── fec: OPTIONAL, OFF by default (no parity on the wire) ─────────────────────
# [fec]
# enabled = true                   # DEFAULT false. Turns the FEC plane on; when
                                   #   false every field below is ignored.
# data_shards = 10                 # K: inner datagrams per coding group. >= 1 when
                                   #   enabled. No default (required).
# parity_shards = 6                # M: parity frames per group (and the adaptive
                                   #   parity CEILING). >= 1 when enabled; K + M <=
                                   #   256. No default (required).
# deadline = "5ms"                 # DEFAULT 5ms. Partial-group flush deadline. > 0
                                   #   and <= 125ms.
# adaptive = false                 # DEFAULT false. Closed-loop parity controller
                                   #   (parity tracks measured loss up to the
                                   #   ceiling). Requires enabled = true.
# target_residual = 0.005          # ADAPTIVE-ONLY, primary sizing surface:
                                   #   post-recovery residual-loss SLA in (0, 1). No
                                   #   default. Mutually exclusive with safety_factor.
# safety_factor = 1.5              # ADAPTIVE-ONLY, legacy headroom multiplier >= 1.
                                   #   DEFAULTS to 1.5 when adaptive and NEITHER field
                                   #   is set. Must stay 0 in fixed mode. Mutually
                                   #   exclusive with target_residual.

# ── dns: OPTIONAL. Omitted => system resolver, default cadence/timeouts ───────
# Selects the TRANSPORT a peer's opt-in hostname endpoint (dns = true, above)
# is resolved through. [dns] alone never turns hostname resolution on.
#
# EXAMPLE 1 (IP-literal DoH resolver):
# [dns]
# resolver = "doh"                 # "system" | "doh" | "dot"
# doh_url = "https://198.51.100.1/dns-query"  # REQUIRED iff resolver = "doh"
# poll_interval = "30s"            # OPTIONAL, DEFAULT 30s. Re-resolution cadence.
# timeout = "5s"                   # OPTIONAL, DEFAULT 5s. Per-lookup timeout.
#
# EXAMPLE 2 (hostname DoT resolver, requires bootstrap_ip):
# [dns]
# resolver = "dot"                 # "system" | "doh" | "dot"
# dot_server = "dns.example.com:853"  # REQUIRED iff resolver = "dot"
# bootstrap_ip = "198.51.100.2"    # REQUIRED: dot_server's host is a hostname
                                   #   (BOOTSTRAP-IP invariant: must reach the
                                   #   resolver WITHOUT DNS lookup itself).
# poll_interval = "30s"
# timeout = "5s"
#
# EXAMPLE 3 (system resolver, any IP-literal endpoint):
# [dns]
# resolver = "system"              # DEFAULT "system". No URL/server/bootstrap needed.
# poll_interval = "30s"
# timeout = "5s"
#
# Each EXAMPLE above is self-contained and independently valid: uncomment
# EXACTLY ONE (its [dns] header plus its fields) and nothing else. The three
# resolver modes are mutually exclusive — setting fields from more than one
# EXAMPLE at once (e.g. resolver = "doh" together with dot_server, or a
# bootstrap_ip on a doh_url/dot_server host that is already an IP literal) is
# rejected at config load. See internal/config/dns.go for every field's exact
# precondition.

# ── metrics: OPTIONAL. Omit the block => no /metrics endpoint is served ───────
[metrics]
listen = "127.0.0.1:9090"          # No default. LOOPBACK-ONLY (127.0.0.0/8, ::1, or
                                   #   a hostname resolving only to loopback); any
                                   #   other address is REFUSED when the endpoint
                                   #   binds (not at load).

# ── log: OPTIONAL ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
[log]
level = "info"                     # DEFAULT "info" (empty => info). One of
                                   #   debug | info | warn | error ("warning"
                                   #   accepted for warn). Unknown => fail-fast.

# ── Cross-field constraints (not expressible inline) ─────────────────────────
# • Roles: `role` is required, never inferred. Edge-only: per-peer endpoint/
#   endpoints (required on edge, rejected on concentrator) + the pacing
#   declarations (dest_addr is edge-only in effect, merely inert on the
#   concentrator). Concentrator-only: wireguard.listen_port (required there).
# • endpoint vs endpoints: mutually exclusive; `endpoint` is the one-element form
#   of `endpoints`. On the edge exactly one must be present; the concentrator sets
#   neither. Each entry is an ip:port literal (default) or, with the peer's
#   `dns = true` opt-in, a hostname:port resolved at runtime (never at config
#   load). Duplicates are rejected within each form (literal-vs-literal,
#   hostname:port-vs-hostname:port) — a hostname is never compared against a
#   literal at load time.
# • Distinct source_addr: each [[paths]] needs a unique, valid source_addr
#   (compared unmapped, so 192.0.2.10 and ::ffff:192.0.2.10 collide). `name` must
#   also be unique.
# • allowed_ips: >= 1 CIDR per peer; empty is rejected when the WG config is
#   assembled (not by config.validate).
# • amnezia all-or-nothing: an unconfigured (all-zero) block is plain WireGuard.
#   Once ANY of jc/jmin/jmax/s1/s2/h1..h4 is set, the full jc,jmin,jmax,s1,s2 set
#   must be > 0, jmin <= jmax, 148+s1 != 92+s2, and h1..h4 must be a distinct set
#   (omitted headers default to 1..4). Same profile on both ends. One engine/process.
# • link_bandwidth + link_rtt are a pair: both-or-neither, and all-or-nothing
#   across every path (declare on every [[paths]] block or none). They take effect
#   ONLY under scheduler.policy = "weighted" with pacing_enabled = true; otherwise
#   a declared bandwidth is inert. When active they are mutually exclusive with the
#   raw per_path_capacity_fps / pacing_burst_frames knobs.
# • scheduler off-unless-present: no [scheduler] => active-backup, every weighted
#   knob ignored. Under weighted, the hysteresis band requires

#   `bind` anywhere is unaffected). "auto" reproduces today's selectDeviceBinds
#   heuristic; "source" and "device" force one behavior unconditionally. See
#   install.md §3b for the VLAN-per-WAN policy-routing case `bind = "source"`
#   exists to fix.

#   timeouts; [dns] only selects the transport a peer's `dns = true` opt-in

#   exactly the one matching `resolver`, the other (and bootstrap_ip under

#   hostname-form doh_url/dot_server host REQUIRES bootstrap_ip; an IP
#   literal host REJECTS a non-empty bootstrap_ip (mode mismatch).

16	// single TUN. An interface left DOWN (or otherwise wedged) fails EVERY subsequent write, so
17	// without a rate limit a write storm would emit one diagnostic record per dropped packet.

20	// underneath — this decorator adds ONE extra, actionable record per interval on top of that,
21	// it never suppresses the engine's own logging.
22	const tunDiagnosticInterval = 30 * time.Second
23	
24	// diagnosingTUN wraps a tun.Device and, on a Write that fails with EIO, emits ONE rate-limited
25	// actionable ERROR naming the probable cause (the interface's link state and MTU) and the
26	// remedy, alongside the raw error for debugging (D39, I3). Left undiagnosed, a write to a DOWN
27	// wanbond0 surfaces only as the engine's generic "input/output error" — accurate but useless to
28	// an operator who has never hit the failure mode before. Every OTHER tun.Device method passes
29	// straight through via the embedded interface; only Write is intercepted.

32		name string
33		log  log.Logger

35		// SIOCGIFFLAGS/SIOCGIFMTU inspection); a test injects a fake clock and a scripted interface
36		// state so it can assert the DOWN/UP wording without a real, privileged network interface.
37		now        func() time.Time

39	
40		mu       sync.Mutex
41		lastDiag time.Time
42	}

45	// (platform-specific) interface-state inspection.
46	func newDiagnosingTUN(dev tun.Device, name string, lg log.Logger) *diagnosingTUN {
47		return &diagnosingTUN{Device: dev, name: name, log: lg, now: time.Now, probeState: ifState}
48	}

50	// Write delegates to the wrapped Device and, on an EIO failure, reports a rate-limited
51	// actionable diagnostic. The original error is returned UNCHANGED: this decorator only adds a
52	// diagnostic side effect, it never masks or alters the engine's own error handling.
53	func (d *diagnosingTUN) Write(bufs [][]byte, offset int) (int, error) {
54		n, err := d.Device.Write(bufs, offset)
55		if err != nil && errors.Is(err, syscall.EIO) {
56			d.diagnoseEIO(err)
57		}

62	// suppresses further diagnostics for tunDiagnosticInterval so a write storm (every dropped
63	// packet failing until the operator acts) produces one record, not a flood.
64	func (d *diagnosingTUN) diagnoseEIO(writeErr error) {
65		d.mu.Lock()
66		now := d.now()
67		if !d.lastDiag.IsZero() && now.Sub(d.lastDiag) < tunDiagnosticInterval {
68			d.mu.Unlock()
69			return
70		}
71		d.lastDiag = now
72		d.mu.Unlock()
73	
74		var errno syscall.Errno
75		_ = errors.As(writeErr, &errno)
76	
77		up, mtu, stateErr := d.probeState(d.name)
78		switch {
79		case stateErr != nil:
80			d.log.Error("TUN write failed with I/O error", "interface", d.name, "raw_error", writeErr.Error(),
81				"errno", int(errno), "state", "unknown",
82				"hint", fmt.Sprintf("could not inspect %s state (%v) — address & bring it up (install.md §4)", d.name, stateErr))
83		case !up:
84			d.log.Error("TUN write failed with I/O error", "interface", d.name, "raw_error", writeErr.Error(),
85				"errno", int(errno), "state", "DOWN", "mtu", mtu,
86				"hint", fmt.Sprintf("%s is DOWN — address & bring it up (install.md §4)", d.name))
87		default:
88			d.log.Error("TUN write failed with I/O error", "interface", d.name, "raw_error", writeErr.Error(),
89				"errno", int(errno), "state", "UP", "mtu", mtu,
90				"hint", fmt.Sprintf("%s is UP (mtu=%d) but writes are still failing — check the link/driver (install.md §4)", d.name, mtu))
91		}
92	}
